tags.text: The MBE, SBE, and UBE bits in mstatus and mstatush are WARL fields

tags.name: norm:mstatus_mstatush_xbe_warl
tags.text: The MBE, SBE, and UBE bits in mstatus and mstatush are WARL fields

tags.name: norm:mstatus_mstatush_xbe_warl
tags.text: The MBE, SBE, and UBE bits in mstatus and mstatush are WARL fields

tags.name: norm:mstatus_mbe_op
tags.text: MBE controls whether non-instruction-fetch memory accesses made from M-mode (assuming mstatus.MPRV=0) are little-endian (MBE=0) or big-endian (MBE=1).

tags.name: norm:mstatus_mstatush_mbe_rst
tags.text: If little-endian memory accesses are supported, the mstatus/mstatush field MBE is reset to 0.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_mstatush_mbe_rst
tags.text: If little-endian memory accesses are supported, the mstatus/mstatush field MBE is reset to 0.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_sbe_op
tags.text: If S-mode is not supported, SBE is read-only 0. Otherwise, SBE controls whether explicit load and store memory accesses made from S-mode are little-endian (SBE=0) or big-endian (SBE=1).

tags.name: norm:mstatus_sbe_implicit
tags.text:
For implicit accesses to supervisor-level memory management data
structures, such as page tables, endianness is always controlled by SBE.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_sbe_implicit
tags.text:
For implicit accesses to supervisor-level memory management data
structures, such as page tables, endianness is always controlled by SBE.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_sbe_change_fence
tags.text:
Since changing SBE alters the implementation’s interpretation of these
data structures, if any such data structures remain in use across a
change to SBE, M-mode software must follow such a change to SBE by
executing an SFENCE.VMA instruction with rs1=x0 and rs2=x0.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_sbe_rocopy
tags.text:
If S-mode is supported, an implementation may make SBE be a read-only
copy of MBE.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_ube_op
tags.text: If U-mode is not supported, UBE is read-only 0. Otherwise, UBE controls whether explicit load and store memory accesses made from U-mode are little-endian (UBE=0) or big-endian (UBE=1).

tags.name: norm:mstatus_ube_rocopy
tags.text:
If U-mode is supported, an implementation may make UBE be a
read-only copy of either MBE or SBE.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tvm_warl_op
tags.text: The TVM (Trap Virtual Memory) bit is a WARL field that supports intercepting supervisor virtual-memory management operations. When TVM=1, attempts to read or write the satp CSR or execute an SFENCE.VMA or SINVAL.VMA instruction while executing in S-mode will raise an illegal-instruction exception. When TVM=0, these operations are permitted in S-mode. TVM is read-only 0 when S-mode is not supported.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tvm_warl_op
tags.text: The TVM (Trap Virtual Memory) bit is a WARL field that supports intercepting supervisor virtual-memory management operations. When TVM=1, attempts to read or write the satp CSR or execute an SFENCE.VMA or SINVAL.VMA instruction while executing in S-mode will raise an illegal-instruction exception. When TVM=0, these operations are permitted in S-mode. TVM is read-only 0 when S-mode is not supported.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tw_warl
tags.text:
The TW (Timeout Wait) bit is a WARL field that supports intercepting the WFI
instruction (see <<wfi>>).

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tw_op
tags.text:
When TW=0, the WFI
instruction may execute in modes less privileged than M when not prevented for
some other reason. When TW=1, then if WFI is executed in any
less-privileged mode, and it does not complete within an
implementation-specific, bounded time limit, the WFI instruction causes
an illegal-instruction exception.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tw_umode_op
tags.text:
When S-mode is implemented, then executing WFI in U-mode causes an
illegal-instruction exception, regardless of the value of the TW bit, unless the
instruction completes within an implementation-specific, bounded time limit.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tw_acc
tags.text: TW is read-only 0 when there are no modes less privileged than M.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tw_op
tags.text:
When TW=0, the WFI
instruction may execute in modes less privileged than M when not prevented for
some other reason. When TW=1, then if WFI is executed in any
less-privileged mode, and it does not complete within an
implementation-specific, bounded time limit, the WFI instruction causes
an illegal-instruction exception.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tw_always_illegal
tags.text:
An implementation may have WFI always
raise an illegal-instruction exception in modes less privileged than M when
TW=1, even if there are pending globally-disabled interrupts when the
instruction is executed.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tsr_warl
tags.text:
The TSR (Trap SRET) bit is a WARL field that supports intercepting the
supervisor exception return instruction, SRET.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tsr_op
tags.text:
When TSR=1, attempts to
execute SRET while executing in S-mode will raise an illegal-instruction
exception. When TSR=0, this operation is permitted in S-mode.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_tsr_acc
tags.text: TSR is read-only 0 when S-mode is not supported.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_vs_warl
tags.text: The FS[1:0] and VS[1:0] WARL fields

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_vs_warl
tags.text: The FS[1:0] and VS[1:0] WARL fields

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_op
tags.text:
The FS field encodes the status of the
floating-point unit state, including the floating-point registers
f0–f31 and the CSRs fcsr, frm, and fflags.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_vs_op
tags.text:
The VS field encodes the status of the
vector extension state, including the vector
registers v0–v31 and the CSRs vcsr, vxrm, vxsat, vstart,
vl, vtype, and vlenb.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_vs_sstatus_vs_op
tags.text: A vector context status field, VS, is added to mstatus[10:9] and shadowed in sstatus[10:9].  It is defined analogously to the floating-point context status field, FS.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_xs_op1
tags.text:
The XS field encodes the status of
additional user-mode extensions and associated state.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_xs_op2
tags.text:
The XS field represents a summary of all extensions' status as shown in
<<norm:mstatus_fs_vs_xs_enc>>.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_vs_xs_enc
tags.text:
Status|FS and VS Meaning|XS Meaning
===
0
1
2
3|Off
Initial
Clean
Dirty|All off
None dirty or clean, some on
None dirty, some clean
Some dirty
===

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_vs_xs_enc
tags.text:
Status|FS and VS Meaning|XS Meaning
===
0
1
2
3|Off
Initial
Clean
Dirty|All off
None dirty or clean, some on
None dirty, some clean
Some dirty
===

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_vs_xs_enc
tags.text:
Status|FS and VS Meaning|XS Meaning
===
0
1
2
3|Off
Initial
Clean
Dirty|All off
None dirty or clean, some on
None dirty, some clean
Some dirty
===

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_acc1
tags.text: If the F extension is implemented, the FS field shall not be read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_acc2
tags.text: If neither the F extension nor S-mode is implemented, then FS is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_rdonly0_s_no_f
tags.text:
If S-mode is implemented but the F extension is not, FS
may optionally be read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_vs_acc1
tags.text: If the v registers are implemented, the VS field shall not be read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_vs_acc2
tags.text:
If neither the v registers nor S-mode is implemented, then VS is
read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_vs_rdonly0_s_no_v
tags.text:
If S-mode is implemented but the v registers are not,
VS may optionally be read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_xs_acc
tags.text:
In harts without additional user extensions requiring new state, the
XS field is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_xs_equiv
tags.text:
Every additional extension with state
provides a CSR field that encodes the equivalent of the XS states.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_sd_acc
tags.text: The SD bit is a read-only bit

tags.name: norm:mstatus_sd_op1
tags.text:
summarizes whether either the FS, VS,
or XS fields signal the presence of some dirty state that will require
saving extended user context to memory.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_sd_op2
tags.text: If mstatus.VS is Dirty, mstatus.SD is 1; otherwise, mstatus.SD is set in accordance with existing specifications.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_sd_rdonly0
tags.text: If FS, XS, and VS are all read-only zero, then SD is also always zero.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_vs_xs_off_op
tags.text:
When an extension's status is set to Off, any instruction that attempts
to read or write the corresponding state will cause an
illegal-instruction exception.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_vs_xs_initial_op
tags.text:
When the status is Initial, the corresponding
state should have an initial constant value.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_vs_xs_clean_op
tags.text:
When the status is Clean,
the corresponding state is potentially different from the initial value,
but matches the last value stored on a context swap.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_vs_xs_dirty_op
tags.text:
When the status is Dirty,
the corresponding state has potentially been modified since the last context save.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_vs_xs_update_indep_priv
tags.text:
The status fields will
also be updated during execution of instructions, regardless of privilege mode.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_wr
tags.text:
Changing the setting of FS has no effect on the contents of the
floating-point register state. In particular, setting FS=Off does not
destroy the state, nor does setting FS=Initial clear the contents.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_vs_wr
tags.text:
the setting of VS has no effect on the contents of the vector
register state.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_imprecise
tags.text:
Implementations may choose to track the dirtiness of the floating-point
register state imprecisely by reporting the state to be dirty even when
it has not been modified.
On some implementations, some instructions
that do not mutate the floating-point state may cause the state to
transition from Initial or Clean to Dirty.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_no_dirty_track
tags.text:
dirtiness might not be tracked at all, in which case the valid FS states
are Off and Dirty, and an attempt to set FS to Initial or Clean causes
it to be set to Dirty.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_no_change_dirty
tags.text:
If an instruction explicitly or implicitly writes a floating-point
register or the fcsr but does not alter its contents, and FS=Initial
or FS=Clean, it is implementation-defined whether FS transitions to
Dirty.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_fs_imprecise
tags.text:
Implementations may choose to track the dirtiness of the floating-point
register state imprecisely by reporting the state to be dirty even when
it has not been modified.
On some implementations, some instructions
that do not mutate the floating-point state may cause the state to
transition from Initial or Clean to Dirty.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_vs_imprecise
tags.text:
Implementations may choose to track the dirtiness of the vector register
state in an analogous imprecise fashion, including possibly setting VS
to Dirty when software attempts to set VS=Initial or VS=Clean.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_vs_no_change_dirty
tags.text:
When VS=Initial or VS=Clean, it is implementation-defined whether an
instruction that writes a vector register or vector CSR but does not
alter its contents causes VS to transition to Dirty.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_spelp_mpelp_op
tags.text:
The Zicfilp extension adds the SPELP and MPELP fields that hold the previous
ELP, and are updated as specified in <<ZICFILP_FORWARD_TRAPS>>.

tags.name: norm:cfi_mstatus_spelp_op
tags.text:
To store the previous ELP state on trap
delivery to S/HS-mode, an SPELP bit is provided in the mstatus CSR.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_spelp_mpelp_op
tags.text:
The Zicfilp extension adds the SPELP and MPELP fields that hold the previous
ELP, and are updated as specified in <<ZICFILP_FORWARD_TRAPS>>.

tags.name: norm:cfi_mstatus_mpelp_op
tags.text:
To store the previous ELP state on trap delivery to M-mode, an MPELP
bit is provided in the mstatus CSR.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_spelp_mpelp_enc_lead-in
tags.text: The x`PELP` fields are encoded as follows:

tags.name: norm:mstatus_spelp_mpelp_enc_list
tags.text:
0 - NO_LP_EXPECTED - no landing pad instruction expected.
1 - LP_EXPECTED - a landing pad instruction is expected.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_spelp_mpelp_enc_lead-in
tags.text: The x`PELP` fields are encoded as follows:

tags.name: norm:mstatus_spelp_mpelp_enc_list
tags.text:
0 - NO_LP_EXPECTED - no landing pad instruction expected.
1 - LP_EXPECTED - a landing pad instruction is expected.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_mdt_clr_mret_sret
tags.text: The MRET and SRET instructions, when executed in M-mode, set the MDT bit to 0.

tags.name: norm:sstatus_sdt_clr_mret_sret
tags.text: If the new privilege mode is U, VS, or VU, then sstatus.SDT is also set to 0.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_mdt_clr_mnret
tags.text: The MNRET instruction, provided by the Smrnmi extension, sets the MDT bit to 0 if the new privilege mode is not M.

tags.name: norm:sstatus_sdt_clr_mnret
tags.text: If it is U, VS, or VU, then sstatus.SDT is also set to 0.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_mdt_clr_mret_sret
tags.text: The MRET and SRET instructions, when executed in M-mode, set the MDT bit to 0.

tags.name: norm:vsstatus_sdt_clr_mret_sret
tags.text: Additionally, if it is VU, then vsstatus.SDT is also set to 0.

tags.name: norm:mstatus_mdt_clr_mnret
tags.text: The MNRET instruction, provided by the Smrnmi extension, sets the MDT bit to 0 if the new privilege mode is not M.

tags.name: norm:vsstatus_sdt_clr_mnret
tags.text: Additionally, if it is VU, then vsstatus.SDT is also set to 0.

tags.name: norm:mtvec_sz_warl_acc
tags.text:
The mtvec register is an MXLEN-bit WARL read/write register that holds
trap vector configuration, consisting of a vector base address (BASE)
and a vector mode (MODE).

tags.name: norm:mtvec_sz_warl_acc
tags.text:
The mtvec register is an MXLEN-bit WARL read/write register that holds
trap vector configuration, consisting of a vector base address (BASE)
and a vector mode (MODE).

tags.name: norm:mtvec_sz_warl_acc
tags.text:
The mtvec register is an MXLEN-bit WARL read/write register that holds
trap vector configuration, consisting of a vector base address (BASE)
and a vector mode (MODE).

tags.name: norm:mtvec_mandatory
tags.text: The mtvec register must always be implemented

tags.name: norm:mtvec_mandatory
tags.text: The mtvec register must always be implemented

tags.name: norm:mtvec_rdonly
tags.text: can contain a read-only value.

tags.name: norm:mtvec_base_align_4B
tags.text:
The value in the BASE field must
always be aligned on a 4-byte boundary

tags.name: norm:mtvec_base_align_func_mode
tags.text:
the MODE setting may impose
additional alignment constraints on the value in the BASE field.

tags.name: norm:mtvec_mode_enc
tags.text:
Value|Name|Description
===
0
1
≥2|Direct
Vectored
---|All traps set pc to BASE.
Asynchronous interrupts set pc to BASE+4×cause.
Reserved
===

tags.name: norm:mtvec_mode_direct_op
tags.text:
When MODE=Direct, all traps into
machine mode cause the pc to be set to the address in the BASE field.

tags.name: norm:mtvec_mode_vectored_op
tags.text:
When MODE=Vectored, all synchronous exceptions into machine mode cause
the pc to be set to the address in the BASE field, whereas interrupts
cause the pc to be set to the address in the BASE field plus four
times the interrupt cause number.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_mideleg_op1
tags.text:
implementations can provide individual read/write bits within medeleg
and mideleg to indicate that certain exceptions and interrupts should
be processed directly by a lower privilege level.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_mideleg_op2
tags.text:
setting a bit in medeleg or mideleg will delegate the
corresponding trap, when occurring in S-mode or U-mode, to the S-mode
trap handler.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_mideleg_op1
tags.text:
implementations can provide individual read/write bits within medeleg
and mideleg to indicate that certain exceptions and interrupts should
be processed directly by a lower privilege level.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_mideleg_op2
tags.text:
setting a bit in medeleg or mideleg will delegate the
corresponding trap, when occurring in S-mode or U-mode, to the S-mode
trap handler.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_enc_txt
tags.text:
medeleg has a bit position allocated for every synchronous exception
shown in <<norm:mcause_exccode_enc_img>>, with the index of the
bit position equal to the value returned in the mcause register

tags.name: norm:mideleg_enc_txt
tags.text:
mideleg holds trap delegation bits for individual interrupts, with the
layout of bits matching those in the mip register

tags.name: norm:medelegh_sz_acc_enc_xlen32
tags.text:
When XLEN=32, medelegh is a 32-bit read/write register
that aliases bits 63:32 of medeleg.

tags.name: norm:medelegh_sz_acc_enc_xlen32
tags.text:
When XLEN=32, medelegh is a 32-bit read/write register
that aliases bits 63:32 of medeleg.

tags.name: norm:medelegh_sz_acc_enc_xlen32
tags.text:
When XLEN=32, medelegh is a 32-bit read/write register
that aliases bits 63:32 of medeleg.

tags.name: norm:medelegh_omit_xlen64
tags.text: The medelegh register does not exist when XLEN=64.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_sz_acc
tags.text: The machine exception delegation register (medeleg) is a 64-bit read/write register.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_sz_acc
tags.text: The machine exception delegation register (medeleg) is a 64-bit read/write register.

tags.name: norm:mideleg_sz_acc
tags.text:
The machine interrupt delegation (mideleg) register is an MXLEN-bit
read/write register.

tags.name: norm:mideleg_sz_acc
tags.text:
The machine interrupt delegation (mideleg) register is an MXLEN-bit
read/write register.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_mideleg_mandatory_S_mode
tags.text:
In harts with S-mode, the medeleg and mideleg registers must
exist

tags.name: norm:medeleg_mideleg_mandatory_S_mode
tags.text:
In harts with S-mode, the medeleg and mideleg registers must
exist

tags.name: norm:medeleg_mideleg_omit_wo_S_mode
tags.text: In harts without S-mode, the medeleg and mideleg registers should not exist.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_mideleg_omit_wo_S_mode
tags.text: In harts without S-mode, the medeleg and mideleg registers should not exist.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_mideleg_warl
tags.text:
An implementation can choose to subset the delegatable traps, with the
supported delegatable bits found by writing one to every bit location,
then reading back the value in medeleg or mideleg to see which bit
positions hold a one.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_mideleg_warl
tags.text:
An implementation can choose to subset the delegatable traps, with the
supported delegatable bits found by writing one to every bit location,
then reading back the value in medeleg or mideleg to see which bit
positions hold a one.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_no_rd1
tags.text: An implementation shall not have any bits of medeleg be read-only one

tags.name: norm:mideleg_no_rd1
tags.text:
an implementation shall not fix as read-only one
any bits of mideleg corresponding to machine-level interrupts

tags.name: norm:mideleg_rd1_lower_level
tags.text: may do so for lower-level interrupts

tags.name: norm:medeleg_when_rd0
tags.text:
For exceptions that cannot occur in less privileged modes, the
corresponding medeleg bits should be read-only zero. In particular,
medeleg[11] is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:medeleg_16_no_rd0
tags.text: The medeleg[16] is read-only zero as double trap is not delegatable.

tags.name: norm:mip_sz_acc
tags.text:
The mip register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register containing
information on pending interrupts

tags.name: norm:mip_sz_acc
tags.text:
The mip register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register containing
information on pending interrupts

tags.name: norm:mie_sz_acc
tags.text:
mie is the corresponding
MXLEN-bit read/write register containing interrupt enable bits.

tags.name: norm:mie_sz_acc
tags.text:
mie is the corresponding
MXLEN-bit read/write register containing interrupt enable bits.

tags.name: norm:mip_mie_enc_txt
tags.text:
Interrupt cause number i (as reported in CSR mcause,
<<mcause>>) corresponds with bit i in both mip and mie.
Bits 15:0 are allocated to standard interrupt causes only, while
bits 16 and above are designated for platform use.

tags.name: norm:mip_mie_enc_txt
tags.text:
Interrupt cause number i (as reported in CSR mcause,
<<mcause>>) corresponds with bit i in both mip and mie.
Bits 15:0 are allocated to standard interrupt causes only, while
bits 16 and above are designated for platform use.

tags.name: norm:mip_mie_std_enc_txt
tags.text:
The standard portions (bits 15:0) of the mip and mie registers are
formatted as shown in <<norm:mip_std_enc_img>> and <<norm:mie_std_enc_img>> respectively.

tags.name: norm:mip_mie_std_enc_txt
tags.text:
The standard portions (bits 15:0) of the mip and mie registers are
formatted as shown in <<norm:mip_std_enc_img>> and <<norm:mie_std_enc_img>> respectively.

tags.name: norm:mip_bits_wr_or_rdonly
tags.text: Each individual bit in register mip may be writable or may be read-only.

tags.name: norm:mip_bits_wr_op
tags.text:
When bit i in mip is writable, a pending interrupt i
can be cleared by writing 0 to this bit.

tags.name: norm:mip_bits_rdonly_op
tags.text:
If interrupt i can become pending but bit i in mip is read-only, the implementation must
provide some other mechanism for clearing the pending interrupt.

tags.name: norm:mie_bits_wr
tags.text: A bit in mie must be writable if the corresponding interrupt can ever become pending.

tags.name: norm:mie_bits_rdonly0
tags.text: Bits of mie that are not writable must be read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:mip_meip_mie_meie_op
tags.text:
Bits mip.MEIP and mie.MEIE are the interrupt-pending and
interrupt-enable bits for machine-level external interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_meip_mie_meie_op
tags.text:
Bits mip.MEIP and mie.MEIE are the interrupt-pending and
interrupt-enable bits for machine-level external interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_meip_rdonly
tags.text:
MEIP is read-only in mip, and is set and cleared by a platform-specific
interrupt controller.

tags.name: norm:mip_mtip_mie_mtie_op
tags.text:
Bits mip.MTIP and mie.MTIE are the interrupt-pending and
interrupt-enable bits for machine timer interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_mtip_mie_mtie_op
tags.text:
Bits mip.MTIP and mie.MTIE are the interrupt-pending and
interrupt-enable bits for machine timer interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_mtip_rdonly
tags.text:
MTIP is read-only in the mip register, and is cleared by writing to
the memory-mapped machine-mode timer compare register.

tags.name: norm:mip_msip_mie_msie_op
tags.text:
Bits mip.MSIP and mie.MSIE are the interrupt-pending and
interrupt-enable bits for machine-level software interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_msip_mie_msie_op
tags.text:
Bits mip.MSIP and mie.MSIE are the interrupt-pending and
interrupt-enable bits for machine-level software interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_msip_rdonly
tags.text:
MSIP is read-only in mip, and is written by accesses to memory-mapped control
registers, which are used to provide machine-level interprocessor interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_msip_mie_msie_maybe_rdonly0
tags.text:
If a system has only one hart, or
if a platform standard supports the delivery of machine-level
interprocessor interrupts through external interrupts (MEI) instead,
then mip.MSIP and mie.MSIE may both be read-only zeros.

tags.name: norm:mip_msip_mie_msie_maybe_rdonly0
tags.text:
If a system has only one hart, or
if a platform standard supports the delivery of machine-level
interprocessor interrupts through external interrupts (MEI) instead,
then mip.MSIP and mie.MSIE may both be read-only zeros.

tags.name: norm:mip_sxip_mie_sxie_rdonly0
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is not implemented, bits SEIP, STIP, and SSIP of

tags.name: norm:mip_sxip_mie_sxie_rdonly0
tags.text:

tags.text:

tags.name: norm:mip_sxip_mie_sxie_rdonly0
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is not implemented, bits SEIP, STIP, and SSIP of
mip and SEIE, STIE, and SSIE of mie are read-only zeros.

tags.name: norm:mip_sxip_mie_sxie_rdonly0
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is not implemented, bits SEIP, STIP, and SSIP of
mip and SEIE, STIE, and SSIE of mie are read-only zeros.

tags.name: norm:mip_sxip_mie_sxie_rdonly0
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is not implemented, bits SEIP, STIP, and SSIP of
mip and SEIE, STIE, and SSIE of mie are read-only zeros.

tags.name: norm:mip_seip_mie_seie_op
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is implemented, bits mip.SEIP and mie.SEIE are
the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level external interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_seip_mie_seie_op
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is implemented, bits mip.SEIP and mie.SEIE are
the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level external interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_seip_acc
tags.text: SEIP is writable in mip

tags.name: norm:mip_seip_rdcsr
tags.text:
When mip is read with a CSR instruction, the value of the
SEIP bit returned in the rd destination register is the logical-OR of
the software-writable bit and the interrupt signal from the interrupt
controller

tags.name: norm:mip_seip_wrcsr
tags.text:
the signal from the interrupt controller is not used to
calculate the value written to SEIP. Only the software-writable SEIP bit
participates in the read-modify-write sequence of a CSRRS or CSRRC instruction.

tags.name: norm:mip_stip_no_stimecmp_acc
tags.text:
If the stimecmp register is not implemented, STIP is writable in
mip

tags.name: norm:mip_stip_mie_stie_op
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is implemented, its mip.STIP and mie.STIE are
the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level timer interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_stip_no_stimecmp_acc
tags.text:
If the stimecmp register is not implemented, STIP is writable in
mip

tags.name: norm:mip_stip_no_stimecmp_op2
tags.text:
may be written by M-mode software to deliver timer interrupts to
S-mode.

tags.name: norm:mip_stip_stimecmp_acc
tags.text:
If the stimecmp (supervisor-mode timer compare) register is
implemented, STIP is read-only in mip

tags.name: norm:mip_stip_mie_stie_op
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is implemented, its mip.STIP and mie.STIE are
the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level timer interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_stip_stimecmp_acc
tags.text:
If the stimecmp (supervisor-mode timer compare) register is
implemented, STIP is read-only in mip

tags.name: norm:mip_stip_stimecmp_op2
tags.text:
reflects the supervisor-level timer
interrupt signal resulting from stimecmp.

tags.name: norm:mip_stip_stimecmp_clr
tags.text:
This timer interrupt signal is
cleared by writing stimecmp with a value greater than the current time value.

tags.name: norm:mip_stip_mie_stie_op
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is implemented, its mip.STIP and mie.STIE are
the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level timer interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_ssip_acc

tags.name: norm:mip_ssip_mie_ssie_op
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is implemented, bits mip.SSIP and mie.SSIE are
the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level software interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_ssip_mie_ssie_op
tags.text:
If supervisor mode is implemented, bits mip.SSIP and mie.SSIE are
the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level software interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_ssip_acc
tags.text: SSIP is writable in mip

tags.name: norm:mip_ssip_intr_ctrl
tags.text:
may also be set to 1
by a platform-specific interrupt controller.

tags.name: norm:mip_lcofip_mie_lcofie_op
tags.text:
If the Sscofpmf extension is implemented, bits mip.LCOFIP and mie.LCOFIE
are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for local-counter-overflow interrupts.

tags.name: norm:mip_lcofip_op2
tags.text:
reflects the occurrence of a local
counter-overflow overflow interrupt request resulting from any of the mhpmeventn.OF bits being set.

tags.name: norm:LCOFIP_op
tags.text:
When an OF bit is set, it eventually, but not necessarily immediately, sets
the LCOFIP bit in the mip/sip registers.

tags.name: norm:mip_lcofip_mie_lcofie_op
tags.text:
If the Sscofpmf extension is implemented, bits mip.LCOFIP and mie.LCOFIE

tags.name: norm:mip_lcofip_acc
tags.text: LCOFIP is read-write in mip

tags.name: norm:mip_lcofip_mie_lcofie_rdonly0
tags.text:

tags.name: norm:msip_sz_acc
tags.text: A hart's memory-mapped msip register is a 32-bit read/write register

tags.name: norm:msip_sz_acc
tags.text: A hart's memory-mapped msip register is a 32-bit read/write register

tags.name: norm:msip_enc
tags.text: bits 31--1 read as zero and bit 0 contains the MSIP bit.

tags.name: norm:msip_update_max_time
tags.text:
When the memory-mapped msip register changes, it is guaranteed to be
reflected in mip.MSIP eventually, but not necessarily immediately.

tags.name: norm:mcycle_op
tags.text:
The mcycle CSR counts the number of clock cycles executed by the processor
core on which the hart is running.

tags.name: norm:mcycle_minstret_sz
tags.text: The mcycle and minstret registers have 64-bit precision on all RV32 and RV64 harts.

tags.name: norm:mcycle_minstret_rst
tags.text:
The counter registers have an arbitrary value after the hart is reset,
and can be written with a given value.

tags.name: norm:mcycle_minstret_wr
tags.text: Any CSR write takes effect after the writing instruction has otherwise completed.

tags.name: norm:mcycle_shared
tags.text:
The mcycle CSR may be
shared between harts on the same core, in which case writes to mcycle
will be visible to those harts.

tags.name: norm:m_mode_perf_monitoring
tags.text: M-mode includes a basic hardware performance-monitoring facility.

tags.name: norm:time_csr_architectural_availability
tags.text:
Implementations can convert reads of the time and timeh CSRs into
loads to the memory-mapped mtime register, or emulate this
functionality on behalf of less-privileged modes in M-mode software.

tags.text:
reads of the mcycleh, minstreth, mhpmcounternh, and mhpmeventnh
CSRs return bits 63-32 of the corresponding register, and writes change only bits 63-32.

reads of the mcycleh, minstreth, mhpmcounternh, and mhpmeventnh

tags.name: norm:mhpmcounter_num
tags.text:
The hardware performance monitor includes 29 additional 64-bit event
counters, mhpmcounter3-mhpmcounter31.

tags.name: norm:mhpmcounter_warl
tags.text: The mhpmcounters are WARL registers

tags.name: norm:mhpmcounter_mandatory
tags.text: All counters should be implemented

tags.name: norm:mhpmcounter_mhpmevent_rdonly0
tags.text: a legal implementation is to make both the counter and its corresponding event selector be read-only 0.

tags.name: norm:mhpmcounter_sz
tags.text: support up to 64 bits of precision on RV32 and RV64.

tags.name: norm:mhpmevent_sz_warl_op
tags.text:
The event selector CSRs,
mhpmevent3-mhpmevent31, are 64-bit WARL registers that control which
event causes the corresponding counter to increment.

tags.name: norm:mhpmevent_sz_warl_op
tags.text:
The event selector CSRs,
mhpmevent3-mhpmevent31, are 64-bit WARL registers that control which
event causes the corresponding counter to increment.

tags.name: norm:mhpmevent_sz_warl_op
tags.text:
The event selector CSRs,
mhpmevent3-mhpmevent31, are 64-bit WARL registers that control which
event causes the corresponding counter to increment.

tags.name: norm:mhpmevent_enc
tags.text:
The meaning of these events is defined by the platform,
but event 0 is defined to mean "no event."

tags.name: norm:mhpmcounter_mhpmevent_rdonly0
tags.text: a legal implementation is to make both the counter and its corresponding event selector be read-only 0.

tags.name: norm:mcounteren_sz
tags.text: The counter-enable mcounteren register is a 32-bit register

tags.name: norm:mcounteren_op
tags.text:
controls the availability of the hardware performance-monitoring
counters to the next-lower privileged mode.

tags.name: norm:mcounteren_inc_inaccessible
tags.text:
The settings in this register only control accessibility. The act of
reading or writing this register does not affect the underlying
counters, which continue to increment even when not accessible.

tags.name: norm:mcounteren_clr_ill_inst_exc
tags.text:
When the CY, TM, IR, or HPMn bit in the mcounteren register is
clear, attempts to read the cycle, time, instret, or
hpmcountern register while executing in S-mode or U-mode will cause an
illegal-instruction exception.

tags.name: norm:mcounteren_set_nxt_priv
tags.text:
When one of these bits is set, access to
the corresponding register is permitted in the next implemented
privilege mode (S-mode if implemented, otherwise U-mode).

tags.name: norm:mcounteren_tm_clr
tags.text:
when the TM bit in the mcounteren register is clear, attempts to
access the stimecmp or vstimecmp register while executing in a mode less
privileged than M will cause an illegal-instruction exception.

tags.name: norm:mcounteren_tm_set
tags.text:
When this bit is set, access to the stimecmp or vstimecmp register is permitted in S-mode
if implemented, and access to the vstimecmp register (via stimecmp) is
permitted in VS-mode if implemented and not otherwise prevented by the TM bit in
hcounteren.

tags.name: norm:mcounteren_flds_mandatory_warl
tags.text:
In harts with U-mode, the mcounteren must be
implemented, but all fields are WARL

tags.name: norm:mcounteren_flds_mandatory_warl
tags.text:
In harts with U-mode, the mcounteren must be
implemented, but all fields are WARL

tags.name: norm:mcounteren_flds_rdonly0
tags.text:
may be read-only zero, indicating reads to the
corresponding counter will cause an illegal-instruction exception when
executing in a less-privileged mode.

tags.name: norm:mcountinhibit_sz_warl_op1
tags.text:
The counter-inhibit register mcountinhibit is a 32-bit
WARL register that controls which of the hardware performance-monitoring counters increment.

tags.name: norm:mcountinhibit_sz_warl_op1
tags.text:
The counter-inhibit register mcountinhibit is a 32-bit
WARL register that controls which of the hardware performance-monitoring counters increment.

tags.name: norm:mcountinhibit_only_inc
tags.text:
The settings in this register only control whether the
counters increment; their accessibility is not affected by the setting
of this register.

tags.name: norm:mcountinhibit_sz_warl_op1
tags.text:
The counter-inhibit register mcountinhibit is a 32-bit
WARL register that controls which of the hardware performance-monitoring counters increment.

tags.name: norm:mcountinhibit_op2
tags.text:
When the CY, IR, or HPMn bit in the mcountinhibit register is clear,
the mcycle, minstret, or mhpmcountern register increments as usual.
When the CY, IR, or HPMn bit is set, the corresponding counter does
not increment.

tags.name: norm:mcountinhibit_cy_shared
tags.text:
The mcycle CSR may be shared between harts on the same core,
in which case the mcountinhibit.CY field is also shared between those harts,
and so writes to mcountinhibit.CY will be visible to those harts.

tags.name: norm:mcountinhibit_not_impl
tags.text:
If the mcountinhibit register is not implemented, the implementation
behaves as though the register were set to zero.

tags.name: norm:mscratch_sz_acc
tags.text:
The mscratch register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register dedicated
for use by machine mode.

tags.name: norm:mscratch_sz_acc
tags.text:
The mscratch register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register dedicated
for use by machine mode.

tags.name: norm:mepc_sz_acc
tags.text: mepc is an MXLEN-bit read/write register

tags.name: norm:mepc_sz_acc
tags.text: mepc is an MXLEN-bit read/write register

tags.name: norm:mepc_align
tags.text: The low bit of mepc (mepc[0]) is always zero. On implementations that support only IALIGN=32, the two low bits (mepc[1:0]) are always zero.

tags.name: norm:mepc_bit1_dyn_ialign_op
tags.text:
If an implementation allows IALIGN to be either 16 or 32 (by changing
CSR misa, for example), then, whenever IALIGN=32, bit mepc[1] is
masked on reads so that it appears to be 0. This masking occurs also for
the implicit read by the MRET instruction. Though masked, mepc[1]
remains writable when IALIGN=32.

tags.name: norm:mepc_warl
tags.text: mepc is a WARL register

tags.name: norm:mepc_inv_addr_conv
tags.text:
Prior to writing mepc, implementations may convert an
invalid address into some other invalid address that mepc is capable of holding.

tags.name: norm:mepc_op
tags.text:
When a trap is taken into M-mode, mepc is written with the virtual
address of the instruction that was interrupted or that encountered the
exception. Otherwise, mepc is never written by the implementation,
though it may be explicitly written by software.

tags.name: norm:mtval_varlen_wr
tags.text:
If mtval is written with a nonzero value when an instruction
access-fault, page-fault, or hardware-error exception occurs on a hart with
variable-length instructions, then mtval will contain the virtual
address of the portion of the instruction that caused the fault

tags.name: norm:mepc_varlen_wr
tags.text: mepc will point to the beginning of the instruction.

tags.name: norm:mcause_sz_acc
tags.text: The mcause register is an MXLEN-bit read-write register

tags.name: norm:mcause_sz_acc
tags.text: The mcause register is an MXLEN-bit read-write register

tags.name: norm:mcause_op
tags.text:
When a trap is taken into
M-mode, mcause is written with a code indicating the event that
caused the trap. Otherwise, mcause is never written by the
implementation, though it may be explicitly written by software.

tags.name: norm:mcause_intr_op
tags.text:
The Interrupt bit in the mcause register is set if the trap was
caused by an interrupt.

tags.name: norm:mcause_exccode_wlrl
tags.text: The Exception Code is a WLRL field, so is only guaranteed to hold supported exception codes.

tags.name: norm:mcause_exccode_ld_ldrsv
tags.text: Note that load and load-reserved instructions generate load exceptions

tags.name: norm:mcause_exccode_st_sc_amo
tags.text:
store, store-conditional, and AMO instructions generate
store/AMO exceptions.

tags.name: norm:mcause_exccode_pri1
tags.text:
If an instruction may raise multiple synchronous exceptions, the
decreasing priority order of <<norm:exc_priority>> indicates which
exception is taken and reported in mcause.

tags.name: norm:mcause_exccode_pri2
tags.text:
Load/store/AMO address-misaligned exceptions may have either higher or
lower priority than load/store/AMO page-fault and access-fault exceptions.

tags.name: norm:mtval_sz_acc
tags.text:
The mtval register is an MXLEN-bit read-write register formatted as
shown in <<norm:mtval_enc_img>>

tags.name: norm:mtval_sz_acc
tags.text:
The mtval register is an MXLEN-bit read-write register formatted as
shown in <<norm:mtval_enc_img>>

tags.name: norm:mtval_vaddr_and_0_sz
tags.text: must be able to hold all valid virtual addresses and the value zero.

tags.name: norm:mtval_instr_bits_sz
tags.text:
If the feature to return the faulting instruction bits is implemented,
mtval must also be able to hold all values less than 2^N^, where
N is the smaller of MXLEN and ILEN.

tags.name: norm:mtval_op
tags.text:
When a trap is taken into M-mode,
mtval is either set to zero or written with exception-specific
information to assist software in handling the trap. Otherwise, mtval
is never written by the implementation, though it may be explicitly
written by software.

tags.name: norm:mtval_per_exc_behavior
tags.text:
The hardware platform will specify which exceptions
must set mtval informatively, which may unconditionally set it to
zero, and which may exhibit either behavior, depending on the underlying event
that caused the exception.

tags.name: norm:mtval_rdonly0
tags.text:
If the hardware platform specifies that no exceptions set mtval
to a nonzero value, then mtval is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:mtval_vaddr_wr1
tags.text:
If mtval is written with a nonzero value when a breakpoint,
address-misaligned, access-fault, page-fault, or hardware-error exception
occurs on an instruction fetch, load, or store, then mtval will contain the
faulting virtual address.

tags.name: norm:mtval_vaddr_not_paddr
tags.text:
When page-based virtual memory is enabled, mtval is written with the
faulting virtual address, even for physical-memory access-fault exceptions.

tags.name: norm:mtval_vaddr_wr2
tags.text:
If mtval is written with a nonzero value when a misaligned load or
store causes an access-fault, page-fault, or hardware-error exception, then mtval will
contain the virtual address of the portion of the access that caused the fault.

tags.name: norm:mtval_varlen_wr
tags.text:
If mtval is written with a nonzero value when an instruction
access-fault, page-fault, or hardware-error exception occurs on a hart with
variable-length instructions, then mtval will contain the virtual
address of the portion of the instruction that caused the fault

tags.name: norm:mtval_instr_bits_lead-in
tags.text:
The mtval register can optionally also be used to return the faulting
instruction bits on an illegal-instruction exception

tags.name: norm:mtval_instr_bits_list
tags.text:
the actual faulting instruction
the first ILEN bits of the faulting instruction
the first MXLEN bits of the faulting instruction

tags.name: norm:mtval_ill_instr_exc_in_low_bits
tags.text:
The value loaded into mtval on an illegal-instruction exception is
right-justified and all unused upper bits are cleared to zero.

tags.name: norm:mtval_swchk_lead-in
tags.text:
On a trap caused by a software-check exception, the mtval register holds
the cause for the exception. The following encodings are defined:

tags.name: norm:mtval_swchk_list
tags.text:
0 - No information provided.
2 - Landing Pad Fault. Defined by the Zicfilp extension ().
3 - Shadow Stack Fault. Defined by the Zicfiss extension ().

tags.name: norm:mtval_other_traps_zero
tags.text: For other traps, mtval is set to zero

tags.name: norm:mtval_warl
tags.text: If mtval is not read-only zero, it is a WARL register

tags.name: norm:mtval_inv_addr_conv
tags.text:
Prior to writing mtval, implementations may convert an invalid address
into some other invalid address that mtval is capable of holding.

tags.name: norm:mconfigptr_sz_acc
tags.text: The mconfigptr register is an MXLEN-bit read-only CSR formatted as shown in <<norm:mconfigptr_enc_img>>

tags.name: norm:mconfigptr_sz_acc
tags.text: The mconfigptr register is an MXLEN-bit read-only CSR formatted as shown in <<norm:mconfigptr_enc_img>>

tags.name: norm:mconfigptr_op
tags.text: holds the physical address of a configuration data structure.

tags.name: norm:mconfigptr_align
tags.text:
The pointer alignment in bits must be no smaller than MXLEN: i.e., if MXLEN is
8{times}n, then mconfigptr[log~2n~-1:0] must be zero.

tags.name: norm:mconfigptr_mandatory
tags.text: The mconfigptr register must be implemented

tags.name: norm:mconfigptr_val
tags.text:
it may be zero to indicate the
configuration data structure does not exist or that an alternative
mechanism must be used to locate it.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_sz_acc
tags.text:
The menvcfg CSR is a 64-bit read/write register, formatted
as shown in <<norm:menvcfg_enc_img>>

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_sz_acc
tags.text:
The menvcfg CSR is a 64-bit read/write register, formatted
as shown in <<norm:menvcfg_enc_img>>

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_op
tags.text:
controls certain characteristics of the execution environment
for modes less privileged than M.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_fiom_fence_op
tags.text:
If bit FIOM (Fence of I/O implies Memory) is set to one in menvcfg,
FENCE instructions executed in modes less privileged than M are modified
so the requirement to order accesses to device I/O implies also the
requirement to order main memory accesses. <<norm:menvcfg_fiom_fence_presuc_op>>
details the modified interpretation of FENCE instruction bits PI, PO,
SI, and SO for modes less privileged than M when FIOM=1.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_fiom_atomic_op
tags.text:
for modes less privileged than M when FIOM=1, if an atomic
instruction that accesses a region ordered as device I/O has its aq
and/or rl bit set, then that instruction is ordered as though it
accesses both device I/O and memory.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_fiom_fence_presuc_op
tags.text:
Instruction bit|Meaning when set
===
PI
PO|Predecessor device input and memory reads (PR implied)
Predecessor device output and memory writes (PW implied)¶SI
SO|Successor device input and memory reads (SR implied)
Successor device output and memory writes (SW implied)
===

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_fiom_rdonly0_ok
tags.text:
If S-mode is not supported, or if satp.MODE is read-only zero (always
Bare), the implementation may make FIOM read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_menvcfgh_no_U_mode
tags.text:
If U-mode is not supported, then registers menvcfg and menvcfgh do
not exist.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_pbmte_op
tags.text:
The PBMTE bit controls whether the Svpbmt extension is available for use
in S-mode and G-stage address translation (i.e., for page tables pointed
to by satp or hgatp). When PBMTE=1, Svpbmt is available for S-mode
and G-stage address translation. When PBMTE=0, the implementation
behaves as though Svpbmt were not implemented.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_pbmte_rdonly0
tags.text: If Svpbmt is not implemented, PBMTE is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_pbmte_henvcfg_pbmte_rdonly0
tags.text:
for implementations with the hypervisor extension,
henvcfg.PBMTE is read-only zero if menvcfg.PBMTE is zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_pbmte_fence
tags.text:
After changing menvcfg.PBMTE, executing an SFENCE.VMA instruction with
rs1=x0 and rs2=x0 suffices to synchronize address-translation caches
with respect to the altered interpretation of page-table entries' PBMT fields.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_adue_op
tags.text:
If the Svadu extension is implemented, the ADUE bit controls whether hardware
updating of PTE A/D bits is enabled for S-mode and G-stage address translations.
When ADUE=1, hardware updating of PTE A/D bits is enabled during S-mode
address translation, and the implementation behaves as though the Svade
extension were not implemented for S-mode address translation.
When the hypervisor extension is implemented, if ADUE=1, hardware updating of
PTE A/D bits is enabled during G-stage address translation, and the
implementation behaves as though the Svade extension were not implemented for
G-stage address translation.
When ADUE=0, the implementation behaves as though Svade were implemented for
S-mode and G-stage address translation.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_adue_rdonly0
tags.text: If Svadu is not implemented, ADUE is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_adue_henvcfg_adue_rdonly0
tags.text:
for implementations with the hypervisor extension,
henvcfg.ADUE is read-only zero if menvcfg.ADUE is zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_adue_fence
tags.text:
After changing menvcfg.ADUE, executing an SFENCE.VMA instruction with
rs1=x0 and rs2=x0 suffices to synchronize address-translation caches
with respect to the altered interpretation of page-table entries' A/D bits.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cde_op
tags.text:
If the Smcdeleg extension is implemented, the CDE (Counter Delegation Enable) bit
controls whether Zicntr and Zihpm counters can be delegated to S-mode. When CDE=1, the Smcdeleg extension is
enabled, see <<smcdeleg>>. When CDE=0, the Smcdeleg and Ssccfg extensions appear to be not implemented.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cde_rdonly0
tags.text: If Smcdeleg is not implemented, CDE is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_stce_op1
tags.text: The Sstc extension adds the STCE (STimecmp Enable) bit to menvcfg CSR.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_stce_op2
tags.text:
The STCE bit enables stimecmp for S-mode when set to one.
When this extension is implemented and STCE in menvcfg is zero, an attempt to access stimecmp
in a mode other than M-mode raises an illegal-instruction exception, STCE in
henvcfg is read-only zero, and STIP in mip and sip reverts to its
defined behavior as if this extension is not implemented. Further, if the H
extension is implemented, then hip.VSTIP also reverts its defined behavior as
if this extension is not implemented.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_stce_rdonly0
tags.text: When the Sstc extension is not implemented, STCE is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cbze_op
tags.text:
The Zicboz extension adds the CBZE (Cache Block Zero instruction enable) field
to menvcfg. When the CBZE field is set to 1, it enables execution of the
cache block zero instruction, CBO.ZERO, in modes less privileged than M.
Otherwise, the instruction raises an illegal-instruction exception in modes less
privileged than M.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cbze_rdonly0
tags.text: When the Zicboz extension is not implemented, CBZE is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cbcfe_op
tags.text:
The Zicbom extension adds the CBCFE (Cache Block Clean and Flush instruction
Enable) field to menvcfg. When the CBCFE field is set to 1, it enables
execution of the cache block clean instruction (CBO.CLEAN) and the cache block
flush instruction (CBO.FLUSH) in modes less privileged than M. Otherwise, these
instructions raise an illegal-instruction exception in modes less privileged than M.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cbcfe_rdonly0
tags.text: When the Zicbom extension is not implemented, CBCFE is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cbie_warl_op
tags.text:
The Zicbom extension adds the CBIE (Cache Block Invalidate instruction Enable)
WARL field to menvcfg to control execution of the cache block invalidate
instruction (CBO.INVAL) in modes less privileged than M. When CBIE is set to
00b, the instruction raises an illegal-instruction exception in modes less
privileged than M.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cbie_warl_op
tags.text:
The Zicbom extension adds the CBIE (Cache Block Invalidate instruction Enable)
WARL field to menvcfg to control execution of the cache block invalidate
instruction (CBO.INVAL) in modes less privileged than M. When CBIE is set to
00b, the instruction raises an illegal-instruction exception in modes less
privileged than M.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cbie_rdonly0
tags.text: When the Zicbom extension is not implemented, CBIE is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cbie_cbo-inval_op_lead-in
tags.text:
When CBIE is set to 01b
or 11b, and when enabled for execution in modes less privileged than M, it
behaves as follows:

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_cbie_cbo-inval_op_list
tags.text:
01b — The instruction is executed and performs a flush operation, even if configured by a mode less privileged than M to perform an invalidate operation.
11b — The instruction is executed and performs an invalidate operation, unless configured by a mode less privileged than M to perform a flush operation.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_pmm_op
tags.text:
If the Smnpm extension is implemented, the PMM field enables or disables
pointer masking (see <<Zpm>>) for the next-lower privilege mode (S-/HS-mode if
S-mode is implemented, or U-mode otherwise), according to the values in
<<norm:menvcfg_pmm_enc>>.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_pmm_rdonly0
tags.text:
If Smnpm is not implemented, PMM is read-only zero.
The PMM field is read-only zero for RV32.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_pmm_enc
tags.text:
Value|Description
===
00|Pointer masking is disabled (PMLEN = 0)¶01|Reserved¶10|Pointer masking is enabled with PMLEN = XLEN - 57 (PMLEN = 7 on RV64)¶11|Pointer masking is enabled with PMLEN = XLEN - 48 (PMLEN = 16 on RV64)
===

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_lpe_op_lead-in
tags.text:
The Zicfilp extension adds the LPE field in menvcfg. When the LPE field is
set to 1 and S-mode is implemented, the Zicfilp extension is enabled in S-mode.
If LPE field is set to 1 and S-mode is not implemented, the Zicfilp extension
is enabled in U-mode. When the LPE field is 0, the Zicfilp extension is not
enabled in S-mode, and the following rules apply to S-mode. If the LPE field
is 0 and S-mode is not implemented, then the same rules apply to U-mode.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_lpe_op_list
tags.text:
The hart does not update the ELP state; it remains as NO_LP_EXPECTED.
The LPAD instruction operates as a no-op.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_sse_op_lead-in
tags.text:
The Zicfiss extension adds the SSE field to menvcfg.
When the SSE field is set to 1 the Zicfiss extension is activated in S-mode. When SSE field is 0,
the following rules apply to privilege modes that are less than M:

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_sse_op_list
tags.text:
32-bit Zicfiss instructions will revert to their behavior as defined by Zimop.
16-bit Zicfiss instructions will revert to their behavior as defined by Zcmop.
The pte.xwr=010b encoding in VS/S-stage page tables becomes reserved.
SSAMOSWAP.W/D raises an illegal-instruction exception.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_sse_rdonly0
tags.text:
When menvcfg.SSE is 0, the henvcfg.SSE and senvcfg.SSE fields are
read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_dte_op
tags.text:
The Ssdbltrp extension adds the double-trap-enable (DTE) field in menvcfg.
When menvcfg.DTE is zero, the implementation behaves as though Ssdbltrp is not
implemented. When Ssdbltrp is not implemented sstatus.SDT, vsstatus.SDT, and
henvcfg.DTE bits are read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:menvcfgh_sz_acc_op
tags.text:
When XLEN=32, menvcfgh is a 32-bit read/write register
that aliases bits 63:32 of menvcfg.
The menvcfgh register does not exist when XLEN=64.

tags.name: norm:menvcfgh_sz_acc_op
tags.text:
When XLEN=32, menvcfgh is a 32-bit read/write register
that aliases bits 63:32 of menvcfg.
The menvcfgh register does not exist when XLEN=64.

tags.name: norm:menvcfgh_sz_acc_op
tags.text:
When XLEN=32, menvcfgh is a 32-bit read/write register
that aliases bits 63:32 of menvcfg.
The menvcfgh register does not exist when XLEN=64.

tags.name: norm:menvcfg_menvcfgh_no_U_mode
tags.text:
If U-mode is not supported, then registers menvcfg and menvcfgh do
not exist.

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_sz_acc
tags.text:
mseccfg is a 64-bit read/write register, formatted as shown in <<norm:mseccfg_enc_img>>,
that controls security features.

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_sz_acc
tags.text:
mseccfg is a 64-bit read/write register, formatted as shown in <<norm:mseccfg_enc_img>>,
that controls security features.

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_presence
tags.text: It exists if any extension that adds a field to mseccfg is implemented. Otherwise, it is reserved.

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_sseed_useed_presence
tags.text: The Zkr extension adds the SSEED and USEED fields to the mseccfg CSR to control access to the seed CSR from modes less privileged than M.

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_sseed_useed_presence
tags.text: The Zkr extension adds the SSEED and USEED fields to the mseccfg CSR to control access to the seed CSR from modes less privileged than M.

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_useed_U-mode_op
tags.text:
When USEED is 0, access to the seed CSR in U-mode raises an
illegal-instruction exception. When USEED is 1, read-write access to the
seed CSR from U-mode is allowed; all other types of accesses raise an
illegal-instruction exception.

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_sseed_useed_op_tbl
tags.text:
Mode|SSEED|USEED|Description
===
M|-|-|The seed CSR is always available in machine
                              mode as normal (with a CSR read-write
                              instruction.) Attempted read without a write
                              raises an illegal-instruction exception
                              regardless of mode and access control bits.¶U|-|0|Any seed CSR access raises an
                              illegal-instruction exception.¶U|-|1|The seed CSR is accessible as normal. No
                              exception is raised for read-write.¶S/HS|0|-|Any seed CSR access raises an
                              illegal-instruction exception.¶S/HS|1|-|The seed CSR is accessible as normal. No
                              exception is raised for read-write.¶VS/VU|0|-|Any seed CSR access raises an
                              illegal-instruction exception.¶VS/VU|1|-|A read-write seed access raises a
                              virtual-instruction exception, while other access
                              conditions raise an illegal-instruction exception.
===

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_useed_rdonly0
tags.text: If Zkr or U-mode is not implemented, USEED is read-only zero.

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_sseed_SorHS-mode_op
tags.text:
When SSEED is 0, access to the seed CSR from S-/HS-mode raises an
illegal-instruction exception. When SSEED is 1, read-write access to the
seed CSR from S-/HS-mode is allowed; all other types of accesses raise an
illegal-instruction exception.

tags.text:

                              mode as normal (with a CSR read-write
                              instruction.) Attempted read without a write
                              raises an illegal-instruction exception
                              regardless of mode and access control bits.¶U|-|0|Any seed CSR access raises an
                              illegal-instruction exception.¶U|-|1|The seed CSR is accessible as normal. No
                              exception is raised for read-write.¶S/HS|0|-|Any seed CSR access raises an
                              illegal-instruction exception.¶S/HS|1|-|The seed CSR is accessible as normal. No
                              exception is raised for read-write.¶VS/VU|0|-|Any seed CSR access raises an
                              illegal-instruction exception.¶VS/VU|1|-|A read-write seed access raises a
                              virtual-instruction exception, while other access
                              conditions raise an illegal-instruction exception.
===

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_sseed_SorHS-mode_op
tags.text:
When SSEED is 0, access to the seed CSR from S-/HS-mode raises an
illegal-instruction exception. When SSEED is 1, read-write access to the
seed CSR from S-/HS-mode is allowed; all other types of accesses raise an
illegal-instruction exception.

                              illegal-instruction exception.¶VS/VU|1|-|A read-write seed access raises a

tags.name: norm:mseccfg_sseed_VSorVU-mode_op
tags.text:
When the H extension is also implemented,
access to the seed CSR from an
HS-qualified instruction leads to a virtual-instruction exception in VS and
VU modes; all other types of accesses raise an illegal-instruction exception.

                              mode as normal (with a CSR read-write
                              instruction.) Attempted read without a write
                              raises an illegal-instruction exception
                              regardless of mode and access control bits.¶U|-|0|Any seed CSR access raises an
                              illegal-instruction exception.¶U|-|1|The seed CSR is accessible as normal. No
                              exception is raised for read-write.¶S/HS|0|-|Any seed CSR access raises an
