a double-precision floating-point number, or vice-versa, respectively

tags.name: norm:fcvt_float_rounding
tags.text:
FCVT.S.D rounds according to the RM field; FCVT.D.S will
never round

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-s_fsgnjn-s_fsgnjx-s_op
tags.text:
Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.S,
FSGNJN.S, and FSGNJX.S, produce a result that takes all bits except the
sign bit from rs1. For FSGNJ, the result's sign bit is rs2's sign
bit; for FSGNJN, the result's sign bit is the opposite of rs2's sign
bit; and for FSGNJX, the sign bit is the XOR of the sign bits of rs1
and rs2. Sign-injection instructions do not set floating-point
exception flags, nor do they canonicalize NaNs

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-q_fsgnjn-q_fsgnjx-q_op
tags.text: Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.Q, FSGNJN.Q, and FSGNJX.Q are defined analogously to the double-precision sign-injection instruction.

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-s_fsgnjn-s_fsgnjx-s_op
tags.text:
Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.S,
FSGNJN.S, and FSGNJX.S, produce a result that takes all bits except the
sign bit from rs1. For FSGNJ, the result's sign bit is rs2's sign
bit; for FSGNJN, the result's sign bit is the opposite of rs2's sign
bit; and for FSGNJX, the sign bit is the XOR of the sign bits of rs1
and rs2. Sign-injection instructions do not set floating-point
exception flags, nor do they canonicalize NaNs

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-q_fsgnjn-q_fsgnjx-q_op
tags.text: Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.Q, FSGNJN.Q, and FSGNJX.Q are defined analogously to the double-precision sign-injection instruction.

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-s_fsgnjn-s_fsgnjx-s_op
tags.text:
Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.S,
FSGNJN.S, and FSGNJX.S, produce a result that takes all bits except the
sign bit from rs1. For FSGNJ, the result's sign bit is rs2's sign
bit; for FSGNJN, the result's sign bit is the opposite of rs2's sign
bit; and for FSGNJX, the sign bit is the XOR of the sign bits of rs1
and rs2. Sign-injection instructions do not set floating-point
exception flags, nor do they canonicalize NaNs

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-q_fsgnjn-q_fsgnjx-q_op
tags.text: Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.Q, FSGNJN.Q, and FSGNJX.Q are defined analogously to the double-precision sign-injection instruction.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_op
tags.text: Floating-point compare instructions (FEQ.S, FLT.S, FLE.S) perform the specified comparison between floating-point registers (rs1 = rs2, rs1 < rs2, rs1 ≤ rs2) writing 1 to the integer register rd if the condition holds, and 0 otherwise.

tags.name: norm:Q_compare_instrs
tags.text: The quad-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their double-precision counterparts, but operate on quad-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_op
tags.text: Floating-point compare instructions (FEQ.S, FLT.S, FLE.S) perform the specified comparison between floating-point registers (rs1 = rs2, rs1 < rs2, rs1 ≤ rs2) writing 1 to the integer register rd if the condition holds, and 0 otherwise.

tags.name: norm:Q_compare_instrs
tags.text: The quad-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their double-precision counterparts, but operate on quad-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_op
tags.text: Floating-point compare instructions (FEQ.S, FLT.S, FLE.S) perform the specified comparison between floating-point registers (rs1 = rs2, rs1 < rs2, rs1 ≤ rs2) writing 1 to the integer register rd if the condition holds, and 0 otherwise.

tags.name: norm:Q_compare_instrs
tags.text: The quad-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their double-precision counterparts, but operate on quad-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_NaN_input
tags.text: For all three instructions, the result is 0 if either operand is NaN

tags.name: norm:feq-s_quiet
tags.text:
FEQ.S performs a quiet
comparison: it only sets the invalid operation exception flag if either input is a signaling NaN

tags.name: norm:Q_compare_instrs
tags.text: The quad-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their double-precision counterparts, but operate on quad-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_NaN_input
tags.text: For all three instructions, the result is 0 if either operand is NaN

tags.name: norm:flt-s_fle-s_signaling
tags.text:
FLT.S and FLE.S perform what the IEEE 754-2008 standard refers to as
signaling comparisons: that is, they set the invalid operation
exception flag if either input is NaN

tags.name: norm:Q_compare_instrs
tags.text: The quad-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their double-precision counterparts, but operate on quad-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_NaN_input
tags.text: For all three instructions, the result is 0 if either operand is NaN

tags.name: norm:flt-s_fle-s_signaling
tags.text:
FLT.S and FLE.S perform what the IEEE 754-2008 standard refers to as
signaling comparisons: that is, they set the invalid operation
exception flag if either input is NaN

tags.name: norm:Q_compare_instrs
tags.text: The quad-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their double-precision counterparts, but operate on quad-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:fclass-s_op
tags.text: The FCLASS.S instruction examines the value in floating-point register rs1 and writes to integer register rd a 10-bit mask that indicates the class of the floating-point number. The format of the mask is described in . The corresponding bit in rd will be set if the property is true and clear otherwise. All other bits in rd are cleared. Note that exactly one bit in rd will be set. FCLASS.S does not set the floating-point exception flags.

tags.name: norm:fclass-q_op
tags.text: The quad-precision floating-point classify instruction, FCLASS.Q, is defined analogously to its double-precision counterpart, but operates on quad-precision operands.

normative_rules.name: Smrnmi_csrs
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:Smrnmi_csrs
tags.text:
The extension adds four new CSRs (mnepc, mncause,
mnstatus, and mnscratch) to hold the interrupted state

normative_rules.name: mnret_exist
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnret_exist
tags.text:
one new
instruction, MNRET, to resume from the RNMI handler.

normative_rules.name: rnmi_priority
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_priority
tags.text:
These interrupts
have higher priority than any other interrupt or exception on the hart

normative_rules.name: rnmi_not_disabled
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_not_disabled
tags.text:
cannot be disabled by software. Specifically, they are not disabled
by clearing the mstatus.MIE register.

normative_rules.name: rnmi_trap_addr
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_trap_addr
tags.text: The RNMI interrupt trap handler address is implementation-defined.

normative_rules.name: rnmi_exc_trap_addr
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_exc_trap_addr
tags.text:
RNMI also has an associated exception trap handler address, which is
implementation defined.

normative_rules.name: mnscratch_sz
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnscratch_sz
tags.text: The mnscratch CSR holds an MXLEN-bit

normative_rules.name: mnscratch_acc
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnscratch_acc
tags.text: read-write register

normative_rules.name: mnscratch_op
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnscratch_op
tags.text:
enables
the RNMI trap handler to save and restore the context that was
interrupted.

normative_rules.name: mnepc_sz
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnepc_sz
tags.text: The mnepc CSR is an MXLEN-bit

normative_rules.name: mnepc_acc
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnepc_acc
tags.text: read-write register

normative_rules.name: mnepc_op
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnepc_op
tags.text:
RNMI trap handler holds the PC of the instruction that took the
interrupt.

normative_rules.name: mnepc_bit0
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnepc_bit0
tags.text: The low bit of mnepc (mnepc[0]) is always zero.

normative_rules.name: mnepc_ialign32
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnepc_ialign32
tags.text:
On implementations
that support only IALIGN=32, the two low bits (mnepc[1:0]) are always
zero.

normative_rules.name: mnepc_ialign_mask
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnepc_ialign_mask
tags.text:
If an implementation allows IALIGN to be either 16 or 32 (by changing
CSR misa, for example), then, whenever IALIGN=32, bit mnepc[1] is
masked on reads so that it appears to be 0.

normative_rules.name: mnepc_bit1_writable
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnepc_bit1_writable
tags.text:
Though masked, mnepc[1]
remains writable when IALIGN=32.

normative_rules.name: MNEPC_WARL
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnepc_warl
tags.text:
mnepc is a WARL register that must be able to hold all valid virtual
addresses.

normative_rules.name: MNCAUSE_WARL
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

normative_rules.name: mnepc_invalid_convert
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnepc_invalid_convert
tags.text:
Prior to writing mnepc, implementations may convert an
invalid address into some other invalid address that mnepc is capable
of holding.

normative_rules.name: mncause_op
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mncause_op
tags.text: The mncause CSR holds the reason for the RNMI.

normative_rules.name: mncause_interrupt
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mncause_interrupt
tags.text: If the reason is an interrupt, bit MXLEN-1 is set to 1,

normative_rules.name: mncause_interrupt_code
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mncause_interrupt_code
tags.text:
the RNMI
cause is encoded in the least-significant bits.

normative_rules.name: mncause_interrupt_zero
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mncause_interrupt_zero
tags.text:
If the reason is an interrupt and RNMI causes are not supported, bit MXLEN-1 is
set to 1, and zero is written to the least-significant bits.

normative_rules.name: mncause_doubletrap
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mncause_doubletrap
tags.text:
If the reason is an exception within M-mode that results in a double trap as
specified in the Smdbltrp extension, bit MXLEN-1 is set to 0 and the
least-significant bits are set to the cause code corresponding to the
exception that precipitated the double trap.

normative_rules.name: MNSTATUS_WARL
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_mnpp_op
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_mnpp_op
tags.text:
The mnstatus CSR holds a two-bit field, MNPP, which on entry to the
RNMI trap handler holds the privilege mode of the interrupted context,
encoded in the same manner as mstatus.MPP.

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_mnpv_op
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_mnpv_op
tags.text:
It also holds a one-bit
field, MNPV, which on entry to the RNMI trap handler holds the virtualization
mode of the interrupted context, encoded in the same manner as
mstatus.MPV.

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_mnpelp_op
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_mnpelp_op
tags.text:
If the Zicfilp extension is implemented, mnstatus also holds the MNPELP
field, which on entry to the RNMI trap handler holds the previous ELP state.

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_mnpelp_update
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_mnpelp_update
tags.text: When an RNMI trap is taken, MNPELP is set to ELP and ELP is set to 0.

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_nmie_enable
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_nmie_enable
tags.text:
When NMIE=1, non-maskable interrupts
are enabled.

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_nmie_disable
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_nmie_disable
tags.text: When NMIE=0, all interrupts are disabled.

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_mprv_clear
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_mprv_clear
tags.text:
When NMIE=0, the hart behaves as though mstatus.MPRV were clear,
regardless of the current setting of mstatus.MPRV.

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_nmie_reset
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_nmie_reset
tags.text: Upon reset, NMIE contains the value 0.

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_nmie_set_clear
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_nmie_set_clear
tags.text: Software can set NMIE to 1, but attempts to clear NMIE have no effect.

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_wfi
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_wfi
tags.text:
For the purposes of the WFI instruction, NMIE is a global interrupt
enable, meaning that the setting of NMIE does not affect the operation
of the WFI instruction.

normative_rules.name: mnstatus_reserved
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnstatus_reserved
tags.text:
The other bits in mnstatus are reserved; software should write zeros
and hardware implementations should return zeros.

normative_rules.name: mnret_mode
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnret_mode
tags.text: MNRET is an M-mode-only instruction

normative_rules.name: mnret_restore
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnret_restore
tags.text:
This instruction also
sets mnstatus.NMIE.

normative_rules.name: mnret_mprv
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnret_mprv
tags.text: If MNRET changes the privilege mode to a mode less privileged than M, it also sets mstatus.MPRV to 0.

normative_rules.name: mnret_zicfilp
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:mnret_zicfilp
tags.text:
If the Zicfilp extension is implemented, then if the new privileged mode
is y, MNRET sets ELP to the logical AND of yLPE (see <<FCFIACT>>) and mnstatus.MNPELP.

normative_rules.name: rnmi_entry
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_entry
tags.text:
When an RNMI interrupt is detected, the interrupted PC is written to the
mnepc CSR,

normative_rules.name: rnmi_entry_cause
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_entry_cause
tags.text: the type of RNMI to the mncause CSR,

normative_rules.name: rnmi_entry_priv
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_entry_priv
tags.text:
the privilege
mode of the interrupted context to the mnstatus CSR.

normative_rules.name: rnmi_entry_nmie_clear
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_entry_nmie_clear
tags.text:
The
mnstatus.NMIE bit is cleared, masking all interrupts.

normative_rules.name: rnmi_enter_mmode
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_enter_mmode
tags.text:
The hart then enters machine-mode and jumps to the RNMI trap handler
address.

normative_rules.name: rnmi_resume
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_resume
tags.text:
The RNMI handler can resume original execution using the new MNRET
instruction, which restores the PC from mnepc, the privilege mode from
mnstatus, and also sets mnstatus.NMIE, which re-enables interrupts.

normative_rules.name: rnmi_exception_nmie0
normative_rules.chapter_name: Smrnmi Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts

tags.name: norm:rnmi_exception_nmie0
tags.text: If the hart encounters an exception while executing in M-mode with the mnstatus.NMIE bit clear, the actions taken are the same as if the exception had occurred while mnstatus.NMIE were set, except that the program counter is set to the RNMI exception trap handler address.

normative_rules.name: rv32i_xreg_sz
normative_rules.summary: RV32I XLEN

tags.name: norm:rv32i_xreg_sz
tags.text:
For RV32I, the 32 x registers are each 32 bits wide,
i.e., XLEN=32.

normative_rules.name: rv32i_other_xregs
normative_rules.summary: Registers x1 to x31

tags.name: norm:rv32i_rv64i_other_xregs
tags.text:
General purpose registers x1-x31 hold values that various
instructions interpret as a collection of Boolean values, or as two's
complement signed binary integers or unsigned binary integers.

normative_rules.name: pcreg_op
normative_rules.description:
The `pc` contains a byte address so is incremented by 4 for 32-bit instructions and
2 for 16-bit instructions.

tags.name: norm:pcreg_op
tags.text: There is one additional unprivileged register: the program counter pc holds the address of the current instruction.

tags.name: norm:slti_sltiu_op
tags.text:
SLTI (set less than immediate) places the value 1 in register rd if
register rs1 is less than the sign-extended immediate when both are
treated as signed numbers, else 0 is written to rd. SLTIU is similar
but compares the values as unsigned numbers (i.e., the immediate is
first sign-extended to XLEN bits then treated as an unsigned number).

tags.name: norm:slti_sltiu_op
tags.text:
SLTI (set less than immediate) places the value 1 in register rd if
register rs1 is less than the sign-extended immediate when both are
treated as signed numbers, else 0 is written to rd. SLTIU is similar
but compares the values as unsigned numbers (i.e., the immediate is
first sign-extended to XLEN bits then treated as an unsigned number).

normative_rules.name: x0eq0
normative_rules.summary: Register x0 always zero

tags.name: norm:x0eq0
tags.text: Register x0 is hardwired with all bits equal to 0.

normative_rules.name: taken_cti_misaligned_exc
normative_rules.summary: CTI misaligned execution

tags.name: norm:taken_cti_misaligned_exc
tags.text:
An instruction-address-misaligned exception is generated on a taken branch
or unconditional jump if the target address is not IALIGN-bit aligned.
This exception is reported on the branch or jump instruction, not on the target instruction.

normative_rules.name: cond_br_no_ia_misaligned_exc_not_taken
normative_rules.summary: No IA misaligned exc on not taken

tags.name: norm:cond_br_no_ia_misaligned_exc_not_taken
tags.text:
No instruction-address-misaligned exception is generated
for a conditional branch that is not taken.

normative_rules.name: imm_always_sex
normative_rules.summary: Immediates always sign-extended

tags.name: norm:imm_always_sex
tags.text:
Except for the 5-bit immediates used in CSR instructions (<<csrinsts>>),
immediates are always sign-extended

tags.name: norm:addi_op
tags.text: ADDI adds the sign-extended 12-bit immediate to register rs1.

normative_rules.name: addi_overflow
normative_rules.summary: Treatment of overflow

tags.name: norm:addi_overflow
tags.text: Arithmetic overflow is ignored and the result is simply the low XLEN bits of the result.

tags.name: norm:andi_ori_xori_op
tags.text:
ANDI, ORI, XORI are logical operations that perform bitwise AND, OR, and
XOR on register rs1 and the sign-extended 12-bit immediate and place
the result in rd.

tags.name: norm:andi_ori_xori_op
tags.text:
ANDI, ORI, XORI are logical operations that perform bitwise AND, OR, and
XOR on register rs1 and the sign-extended 12-bit immediate and place
the result in rd.

tags.name: norm:andi_ori_xori_op
tags.text:
ANDI, ORI, XORI are logical operations that perform bitwise AND, OR, and
XOR on register rs1 and the sign-extended 12-bit immediate and place
the result in rd.

tags.name: norm:slli_op
tags.text: SLLI is a logical left shift (zeros are shifted into the lower bits);

tags.name: norm:srli_op
tags.text: SRLI is a logical right shift (zeros are shifted into the upper bits);

tags.name: norm:srai_op
tags.text: SRAI is an arithmetic right shift (the original sign bit is copied into the vacated upper bits).

tags.name: norm:lui_op
tags.text:
LUI places the 32-bit U-immediate value into the
destination register rd, filling in the lowest 12 bits with zeros.

tags.name: norm:auipc_op
tags.text:
AUIPC forms a 32-bit offset from
the U-immediate, filling in the lowest 12 bits with zeros, adds this
offset to the address of the AUIPC instruction, then places the result
in register rd.

tags.name: norm:R-type_operands
tags.text:
All operations read
the rs1 and rs2 registers as source operands and write the result into register rd.

tags.name: norm:add_op
tags.text: ADD performs the addition of rs1 and rs2.

tags.name: norm:sub_op
tags.text: SUB performs the subtraction of rs2 from rs1.

tags.name: norm:add_sub_overflow
tags.text: Overflows are ignored and the low XLEN bits of results are written to the destination rd.

tags.name: norm:add_sub_overflow
tags.text: Overflows are ignored and the low XLEN bits of results are written to the destination rd.

tags.name: norm:slt_sltu_op
tags.text:
SLT and SLTU perform signed and unsigned compares respectively, writing 1 to rd if
rs1 < rs2, 0 otherwise.

tags.name: norm:slt_sltu_op
tags.text:
SLT and SLTU perform signed and unsigned compares respectively, writing 1 to rd if
rs1 < rs2, 0 otherwise.

tags.name: norm:and_or_xor_op
tags.text: AND, OR, and XOR perform bitwise logical operations.

tags.name: norm:and_or_xor_op
tags.text: AND, OR, and XOR perform bitwise logical operations.

tags.name: norm:and_or_xor_op
tags.text: AND, OR, and XOR perform bitwise logical operations.

tags.name: norm:sll_srl_sra_op
tags.text:
SLL, SRL, and SRA perform logical left, logical right, and arithmetic
right shifts on the value in register rs1 by the shift amount held in
the lower 5 bits of register rs2.

tags.name: norm:sll_srl_sra_op
tags.text:
SLL, SRL, and SRA perform logical left, logical right, and arithmetic
right shifts on the value in register rs1 by the shift amount held in
the lower 5 bits of register rs2.

tags.name: norm:sll_srl_sra_op
tags.text:
SLL, SRL, and SRA perform logical left, logical right, and arithmetic
right shifts on the value in register rs1 by the shift amount held in
the lower 5 bits of register rs2.

tags.name: norm:no_cti_delay_slots
tags.text:
Control transfer instructions in RV32I
do not have architecturally visible delay slots.

tags.name: norm:ia_fault_exc_on_target
tags.text:
If an instruction access-fault or instruction page-fault exception
occurs on the target of a jump or taken branch, the exception is
reported on the target instruction, not on the jump or branch instruction.

tags.name: norm:jal_target
tags.text:
The offset is sign-extended and added to the address of the jump instruction to
form the jump target address.

tags.name: norm:jal_op
tags.text:
JAL stores the address of the instruction
following the jump ('pc'+4) into register rd.

tags.name: norm:jalr_target
tags.text:
The target address is obtained by adding the
sign-extended 12-bit I-immediate to the register rs1, then setting the
least-significant bit of the result to zero.

tags.name: norm:jalr_op
tags.text:
The address of the
instruction following the jump (pc+4) is written to register rd.

tags.name: norm:jump_misaligned_exception
tags.text: The JAL and JALR instructions will generate an instruction-address-misaligned exception if the target address is not aligned to a four-byte boundary.

tags.name: norm:jump_misaligned_c_no_exception
tags.text: Instruction-address-misaligned exceptions are not possible on machines with IALIGN=16, e.g., those with the compressed instruction-set extension, C.

tags.name: norm:br_target
tags.text:
The 12-bit B-immediate encodes signed offsets in multiples of 2 bytes. The offset
is sign-extended and added to the address of the branch instruction to give the target address.

tags.name: norm:beq_bne_op
tags.text: BEQ and BNE take the branch if registers rs1 and rs2 are equal or unequal respectively.

tags.name: norm:beq_bne_op
tags.text: BEQ and BNE take the branch if registers rs1 and rs2 are equal or unequal respectively.

tags.name: norm:blt_bltu_op
tags.text:
BLT and BLTU take the branch if rs1 is less than rs2, using signed and
unsigned comparison respectively.

tags.name: norm:blt_bltu_op
tags.text:
BLT and BLTU take the branch if rs1 is less than rs2, using signed and
unsigned comparison respectively.

tags.name: norm:bge_bgeu_op
tags.text:
BGE and BGEU take the branch if rs1 is greater than or equal to rs2,
using signed and unsigned comparison respectively.

tags.name: norm:bge_bgeu_op
tags.text:
BGE and BGEU take the branch if rs1 is greater than or equal to rs2,
using signed and unsigned comparison respectively.

tags.name: norm:branch_misaligned_taken_exception
tags.text:
The conditional branch instructions will generate an
instruction-address-misaligned exception if the target address is not
aligned to a four-byte boundary and the branch condition evaluates to
true.

tags.name: norm:branch_misaligned_untaken_exception
tags.text:
If the branch condition evaluates to false, the
instruction-address-misaligned exception will not be raised.

tags.name: norm:branch_misaligned_c_no_exception
tags.text: Instruction-address-misaligned exceptions are not possible on machines with IALIGN=16, e.g., those with the compressed instruction-set extension, C.

tags.name: norm:load_exc_x0
tags.text:
Loads with a destination of
x0 must still raise any exceptions and cause any other side effects
even though the load value is discarded.

tags.name: norm:ENDIANNESS_LITTLE_OR_BIG
tags.text: The EEI will define whether the memory system is little-endian or big-endian.

tags.name: norm:ldst_endian_byte_invariant
tags.text: In RISC-V, endianness is byte-address invariant.

tags.name: norm:ldst_endian_byte_op
tags.text: In a system for which endianness is byte-address invariant, the following property holds: if a byte is stored to memory at some address in some endianness, then a byte-sized load from that address in any endianness returns the stored value.

tags.name: norm:ldst_little_endian_op
tags.text: In a little-endian configuration, multibyte stores write the least-significant register byte at the lowest memory byte address, followed by the other register bytes in ascending order of their significance. Loads similarly transfer the contents of the lesser memory byte addresses to the less-significant register bytes.

tags.name: norm:ldst_big_endian_op
tags.text: In a big-endian configuration, multibyte stores write the most-significant register byte at the lowest memory byte address, followed by the other register bytes in descending order of their significance. Loads similarly transfer the contents of the greater memory byte addresses to the less-significant register bytes.

tags.name: norm:ldst_ea
tags.text:
The effective address is obtained by adding register rs1 to the
sign-extended 12-bit offset.

tags.name: norm:ldst_no_exc_aligned
tags.text:
loads and stores whose effective addresses are
naturally aligned shall not raise an address-misaligned exception.

tags.name: norm:load_op
tags.text: Loads copy a value from memory to register rd.

tags.name: norm:store_op
tags.text: Stores copy the value in register rs2 to memory.

tags.name: norm:lw_op
tags.text: The LW instruction loads a 32-bit value from memory into rd.

tags.name: norm:lh_op
tags.text:
LH loads a 16-bit value from memory, then sign-extends to 32-bits before storing
in rd.

tags.name: norm:lhu_op
tags.text:
LHU loads a 16-bit value from memory but then zero extends to
32-bits before storing in rd.

tags.name: norm:lb_lbu_op
tags.text: LB and LBU are defined analogously for 8-bit values.

tags.name: norm:lh_op
tags.text:
LH loads a 16-bit value from memory, then sign-extends to 32-bits before storing
in rd.

tags.name: norm:lb_lbu_op
tags.text: LB and LBU are defined analogously for 8-bit values.

tags.name: norm:lhu_op
tags.text:
LHU loads a 16-bit value from memory but then zero extends to
32-bits before storing in rd.

tags.name: norm:sw_sh_sb_op
tags.text:
The SW, SH, and SB instructions store 32-bit, 16-bit, and
8-bit values from the low bits of register rs2 to memory.

tags.name: norm:sw_sh_sb_op
tags.text:
The SW, SH, and SB instructions store 32-bit, 16-bit, and
8-bit values from the low bits of register rs2 to memory.

tags.name: norm:sw_sh_sb_op
tags.text:
The SW, SH, and SB instructions store 32-bit, 16-bit, and
8-bit values from the low bits of register rs2 to memory.

tags.name: norm:misaligned_ldst_eei_dependent_behavior
tags.text:
Loads and stores whose effective address is not naturally aligned to the
referenced datatype (i.e., the effective address is not divisible by the
size of the access in bytes) have behavior dependent on the EEI.

tags.name: norm:misaligned_ldst_hw_support
tags.text:
misaligned loads and stores can be handled in hardware, or
via an invisible trap into the execution environment implementation, or possibly a
combination of hardware and invisible trap depending on address.

tags.name: norm:misaligned_ldst_exc_type
tags.text:
loads and stores that are not naturally aligned
may either complete execution successfully or raise an exception. The exception raised can be either an
address-misaligned exception or an access-fault exception.
For a memory access that would otherwise be able
to complete except for the misalignment, an access-fault exception can
be raised instead of an address-misaligned exception if the misaligned
access should not be emulated, e.g., if accesses to the memory region
have side effects.

tags.name: norm:misaligned_ldst_contained_or_fatal_trap
tags.text:
When an EEI does not guarantee misaligned loads and
stores are handled invisibly, the EEI must define if exceptions caused
by address misalignment result in a contained trap (allowing software
running inside the execution environment to handle the trap) or a fatal
trap (terminating execution).

tags.name: norm:ldst_atomicity_for_aligned
tags.text:
naturally aligned
loads and stores are guaranteed to execute atomically

tags.name: norm:fence_op
tags.text:
A FENCE
(with fm=0000) orders all memory operations in its predecessor set
before all memory operations in its successor set.

tags.name: norm:fence-tso_op
tags.text:
FENCE.TSO orders
all load operations in its predecessor set before all memory operations
in its successor set, and all store operations in its predecessor set
before all store operations in its successor set. This leaves non-AMO
store operations in the FENCE.TSO's predecessor set unordered with
non-AMO loads in its successor set.

tags.name: norm:fence-tso_ordering_rw_rw_ok
tags.text: Because FENCE RW,RW imposes a superset of the orderings that FENCE.TSO imposes, it is correct to ignore the fm field and implement FENCE.TSO as FENCE RW,RW.

tags.name: norm:fence_unused_flds_rsv
tags.text:
The unused fields in the FENCE
instructions--rs1 and rd--are reserved
for finer-grain fences in future extensions. For forward compatibility,
base implementations shall ignore these fields

tags.name: norm:fence_cons_ok
tags.text: We chose a relaxed memory model to allow high performance from simple machine implementations and from likely future coprocessor or accelerator extensions. We separate out I/O ordering from memory R/W ordering to avoid unnecessary serialization within a device-driver hart and also to support alternative non-memory paths to control added coprocessors or I/O devices. Simple implementations may additionally ignore the predecessor and successor fields and always execute a conservative FENCE on all operations.

tags.name: norm:ecall_op1
tags.text: The ECALL instruction is used to make a service request to the execution environment.

tags.name: norm:ecall_op2
tags.text: The ECALL instruction is used to make a request to the supporting execution environment. When executed in U-mode, S-mode, or M-mode, it generates an environment-call-from-U-mode exception, environment-call-from-S-mode exception, or environment-call-from-M-mode exception, respectively, and performs no other operation.

tags.name: norm:ebreak_op1
tags.text: The EBREAK instruction is used to return control to a debugging environment.

tags.name: norm:ebreak_op2
tags.text: The EBREAK instruction is used by debuggers to cause control to be transferred back to a debugging environment. Unless overridden by an external debug environment, EBREAK raises a breakpoint exception and performs no other operation.

tags.name: norm:hints_op
tags.text:
HINTs do not change any architecturally
visible state, except for advancing the pc and any applicable
performance counters. Implementations are always allowed to ignore the
encoded hints.

tags.name: norm:hints_enc
tags.text:
Most RV32I HINTs are encoded as integer computational instructions with
rd=x0. The other RV32I HINTs are encoded as FENCE instructions with
a null predecessor or successor set and with fm=0.

tags.name: norm:nop_op
tags.text:
The NOP instruction does not change any architecturally visible state,
except for advancing the pc and incrementing any applicable
performance counters.

tags.name: norm:nop_enc
tags.text: NOP is encoded as ADDI x0, x0, 0.

tags.name: norm:diff_rv32e_rv32i
tags.text: RV32E and RV64E use the same instruction-set encoding as RV32I and RV64I respectively, except that only registers x0-x15 are provided. All encodings specifying the other registers x16-x31 are reserved.

normative_rules.name: rv64i_xreg_sz
normative_rules.summary: RV64I XLEN

tags.name: norm:rv64i_xreg_sz
tags.text:
RV64I widens the integer registers and supported user address space to
64 bits (XLEN=64 in <<gprs>>).

tags.name: norm:rv64_w_sex
tags.text:
These "*W"
instructions ignore the upper 32 bits of their inputs and always produce
32-bit signed values, sign-extending them to 64 bits, i.e. bits XLEN-1
through 31 are equal.

normative_rules.name: rv64i_other_xregs
normative_rules.summary: Registers x1 to x31

tags.name: norm:rv32i_rv64i_other_xregs
tags.text:
General purpose registers x1-x31 hold values that various
instructions interpret as a collection of Boolean values, or as two's
complement signed binary integers or unsigned binary integers.

tags.name: norm:addiw_op
tags.text:
ADDIW is an RV64I instruction that adds the sign-extended 12-bit
immediate to register rs1 and produces the proper sign extension of a
32-bit result in rd.

tags.name: norm:slliw_srliw_sraiw_op
tags.text:
SLLIW, SRLIW, and SRAIW are RV64I-only instructions that are analogously
defined but operate on 32-bit values and sign-extend their 32-bit
results to 64 bits.

tags.name: norm:slliw_srliw_sraiw_op
tags.text:
SLLIW, SRLIW, and SRAIW are RV64I-only instructions that are analogously
defined but operate on 32-bit values and sign-extend their 32-bit
results to 64 bits.

tags.name: norm:slliw_srliw_sraiw_op
tags.text:
SLLIW, SRLIW, and SRAIW are RV64I-only instructions that are analogously
defined but operate on 32-bit values and sign-extend their 32-bit
results to 64 bits.

normative_rules.name: slliw_imm5_rsv
normative_rules.summary: Bit 5 of imm reserved

tags.name: norm:slliw_srliw_sraiw_imm5_rsv
tags.text:
SLLIW, SRLIW, and SRAIW encodings with
imm[5] {ne} 0 are reserved.

normative_rules.name: srliw_imm5_rsv
normative_rules.summary: Bit 5 of imm reserved

tags.name: norm:slliw_srliw_sraiw_imm5_rsv
tags.text:

imm[5] {ne} 0 are reserved.

normative_rules.name: sraiw_imm5_rsv
normative_rules.summary: Bit 5 of imm reserved

tags.name: norm:slliw_srliw_sraiw_imm5_rsv
tags.text:
SLLIW, SRLIW, and SRAIW encodings with
imm[5] {ne} 0 are reserved.

tags.name: norm:lui_op_rv64i
tags.text:
LUI places the
32-bit U-immediate into register rd, filling in the lowest 12 bits
with zeros. The 32-bit result is sign-extended to 64 bits.

tags.name: norm:auipc_op_rv64i
tags.text:
AUIPC forms a 32-bit offset from the U-immediate, filling in the lowest
12 bits with zeros, sign-extends the result to 64 bits, adds it to the
address of the AUIPC instruction, then places the result in register rd.

tags.name: norm:addw_subw_op
tags.text:
ADDW and SUBW are RV64I-only instructions that are defined analogously
to ADD and SUB but operate on 32-bit values and produce signed 32-bit results.

tags.name: norm:addw_subw_op
tags.text:
ADDW and SUBW are RV64I-only instructions that are defined analogously
to ADD and SUB but operate on 32-bit values and produce signed 32-bit results.

tags.name: norm:addw_subw_overflow
tags.text:
Overflows are ignored, and the low 32-bits of the result is
sign-extended to 64-bits and written to the destination register.

tags.name: norm:addw_subw_overflow
tags.text:
Overflows are ignored, and the low 32-bits of the result is
sign-extended to 64-bits and written to the destination register.

tags.name: norm:addiw_overflow
tags.text:
Overflows are ignored and the result is the low
32 bits of the result sign-extended to 64 bits.

tags.name: norm:sll_srl_sra_sh_amt_rv64i
tags.text:
In RV64I, only the low 6 bits of rs2 are considered
for the shift amount.

tags.name: norm:sll_srl_sra_sh_amt_rv64i
tags.text:
In RV64I, only the low 6 bits of rs2 are considered
for the shift amount.

tags.name: norm:sll_srl_sra_sh_amt_rv64i
tags.text:
In RV64I, only the low 6 bits of rs2 are considered
for the shift amount.

tags.name: norm:sllw_srlw_sraw_op
tags.text:
SLLW, SRLW, and SRAW are RV64I-only instructions that are analogously
defined but operate on 32-bit values and sign-extend their 32-bit
results to 64 bits. The shift amount is given by rs2[4:0].

tags.name: norm:sllw_srlw_sraw_op
tags.text:
SLLW, SRLW, and SRAW are RV64I-only instructions that are analogously
defined but operate on 32-bit values and sign-extend their 32-bit
results to 64 bits. The shift amount is given by rs2[4:0].

tags.name: norm:sllw_srlw_sraw_op
tags.text:
SLLW, SRLW, and SRAW are RV64I-only instructions that are analogously
defined but operate on 32-bit values and sign-extend their 32-bit

tags.name: norm:ld_op_rv64i
tags.text:
The LD instruction loads a 64-bit value from memory into register rd
for RV64I.

tags.name: norm:lw_op_rv64i
tags.text:
The LW instruction loads a 32-bit value from memory and sign-extends
this to 64 bits before storing it in register rd for RV64I.

tags.name: norm:lwu_op
tags.text:
The LWU instruction, on the other hand, zero-extends the 32-bit value from
memory for RV64I.

tags.name: norm:lh_lhu_lb_lbu_op_rv64i
tags.text:

as are LB and LBU for 8-bit values.

tags.name: norm:sd_sw_sh_sb_op_rv64i
tags.text:
The SD, SW, SH, and SB instructions
store 64-bit, 32-bit, 16-bit, and 8-bit values from the low bits of
register rs2 to memory respectively.

tags.name: norm:sd_sw_sh_sb_op_rv64i
tags.text:
The SD, SW, SH, and SB instructions
store 64-bit, 32-bit, 16-bit, and 8-bit values from the low bits of
register rs2 to memory respectively.

tags.name: norm:sd_sw_sh_sb_op_rv64i
tags.text:
The SD, SW, SH, and SB instructions
store 64-bit, 32-bit, 16-bit, and 8-bit values from the low bits of
register rs2 to memory respectively.

tags.name: norm:sd_sw_sh_sb_op_rv64i
tags.text:
The SD, SW, SH, and SB instructions
store 64-bit, 32-bit, 16-bit, and 8-bit values from the low bits of
register rs2 to memory respectively.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_single_hart_op
tags.text:
Under RVWMO, code running on a single hart appears to execute in order
from the perspective of other memory instructions in the same hart, but
memory instructions from another hart may observe the memory
instructions from the first hart being executed in a different order.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_only_mainmem
tags.text:
This chapter defines the memory model for regular main memory
operations. The interaction of the memory model with I/O memory,
instruction fetches, FENCE.I, page-table walks, and SFENCE.VMA is not
(yet) formalized.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_excludes_overlap_widths
tags.text:
Memory consistency models supporting overlapping
memory accesses of different widths simultaneously remain an active area of academic
research and are not yet fully understood. The specifics of how memory
accesses of different sizes interact under RVWMO are specified to the
best of our current abilities

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_single_copy_atomic
tags.text:
All memory operations are single-copy atomic:
they can never be observed in a partially complete state.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_aligned_memop
tags.text:
Each aligned memory instruction that accesses XLEN or
fewer bits gives rise to exactly one memory operation, unless specified otherwise.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_aligned_amo_memop
tags.text:
An aligned AMO gives rise to a single memory operation that
is both a load operation and a store operation simultaneously.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_sc_fail_no_memop
tags.text: An unsuccessful SC instruction does not give rise to any memory operations.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_v_p_multi_memops
tags.text:
ISA extensions such as V (Vector) and the upcoming P (SIMD)
may give rise to multiple memory operations.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_order_multi_memops
tags.text:
The memory operations generated by such instructions
are not ordered with respect to each other in program order, but they are ordered normally with
respect to the memory operations generated by preceding and subsequent
instructions in program order.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_v_p_multi_memops
tags.text:
ISA extensions such as V (Vector) and the upcoming P (SIMD)
may give rise to multiple memory operations.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_order_multi_memops
tags.text:
The memory operations generated by such instructions
are not ordered with respect to each other in program order, but they are ordered normally with
respect to the memory operations generated by preceding and subsequent
instructions in program order.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_misaligned_ldst_memops
tags.text:
A misaligned load or store instruction may be decomposed
into a set of component memory operations of any granularity.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_order_multi_memops
tags.text:
The memory operations generated by such instructions
are not ordered with respect to each other in program order, but they are ordered normally with
respect to the memory operations generated by preceding and subsequent
instructions in program order.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_fp_gt_xlen_memops
tags.text:
A floating-point load or store of more than XLEN bits may
also be decomposed into a set of component memory operations of any granularity.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_order_multi_memops
tags.text:
The memory operations generated by such instructions
are not ordered with respect to each other in program order, but they are ordered normally with
respect to the memory operations generated by preceding and subsequent
instructions in program order.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_misaligned_in_atomic_pma
tags.text:
if misaligned atomics are supported via the
misaligned atomicity granule PMA, then AMOs within an atomicity granule are not decomposed, nor are
loads and stores defined in the base ISAs, nor are loads and stores of no more
than XLEN bits defined in the F, D, and Q extensions.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_obeyed
tags.text: An execution of a RISC-V program obeys the RVWMO memory consistency model only if there exists a global memory order conforming to preserved program order and satisfying the load value axiom, the atomicity axiom, and the progress axiom.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_lead-in
tags.text: The complete definition of preserved program order is as follows (and note that AMOs are simultaneously both loads and stores): memory operation a precedes memory operation b in preserved program order (and hence also in the global memory order) if a precedes b in program order, a and b both access regular main memory (rather than I/O regions), and any of the following hold:

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_overlap
tags.text: Overlapping-Address Orderings

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_overlap1
tags.text: b is a store, and a and b access overlapping memory addresses

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_overlap2
tags.text:
a and b are loads, x is a byte read by both a and
b, there is no store to x between a and b in program order, and a and b return values for x
written by different memory operations

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_overlap3
tags.text:
a is generated by an AMO or SC instruction, b is a load, and
b returns a value written by a

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_sync
tags.text: Explicit Synchronization

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_sync1
tags.text: There is a FENCE instruction that orders a before b

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_sync2
tags.text: a has an acquire annotation annotation

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_sync3
tags.text: b has a release annotation

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_sync4
tags.text: a and b both have RCsc annotations

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_sync5
tags.text: a is paired with b

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_dep
tags.text: Syntactic Dependencies

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_dep1
tags.text: b has a syntactic address dependency on a

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_dep2
tags.text: b has a syntactic data dependency on a

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_dep3
tags.text: b is a store, and b has a syntactic control dependency on a

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_pipe
tags.text: Pipeline Dependencies

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_pipe1
tags.text:
b is a load, and there exists some store m between a and b
in program order such that m has an address or data dependency on a,
and b returns a value written by m

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ppo_pipe2
tags.text:
b is a store, and there exists some instruction m between a
and b in program order such that m has an address dependency on a

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ld_val_axiom_lead-in
tags.text: Each byte of each load i returns the value written to that byte by the store that is the latest in global memory order among the following stores:

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_ld_val_axiom_list
tags.text:
Stores that write that byte and that precede i in the global memory order
Stores that write that byte and that precede i in program order

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_atomicity_axiom
tags.text: If r and w are paired load and store operations generated by aligned LR and SC instructions in a hart h, s is a store to byte x, and r returns a value written by s, then s must precede w in the global memory order, and there can be no store from a hart other than h to byte x following s and preceding w in the global memory order.

tags.name: norm:rvwmo_progress_axiom
tags.text: No memory operation may be preceded in the global memory order by an infinite sequence of other memory operations.

tags.name: norm:zkr_seed_addr
tags.text: The entropy source extension defines the seed CSR at address 0x015. This CSR provides up to 16 physical entropy bits that can be used to seed cryptographic random bit generators.

tags.name: norm:zkn_impl_all
tags.text: A core which implements Zkn must implement all of the above extensions.

tags.name: norm:zks_impl_all
tags.text: A core which implements Zks must implement all of the above extensions.

tags.name: norm:zk_impl_all
tags.text: A core which implements Zk must implement all of the above extensions.

tags.name: norm:zk_scalar_xreg_op
tags.text:
All instructions described herein use the general-purpose X
registers, and obey the 2-read-1-write register access constraint.

tags.name: norm:zknd_shared_instr
tags.text:

present in both the <<zknd>> and <<zkne>> extensions.
