tags.name: norm:vandn_op
tags.text: Each bit of Op1 is inverted and logically ANDed with the corresponding bits in vs2.

tags.name: norm:vandn-vx_rs1
tags.text:
In the vector-scalar version, Op1 is the sign-extended or truncated value in scalar
register rs1.

tags.name: norm:vandn-vv_vs1
tags.text: In the vector-vector version, Op1 is vs1.

tags.name: norm:vbrev-v_op
tags.text: A bit reversal is performed on the bits of each element.

tags.name: norm:vbrev8-v_op
tags.text: A bit reversal is performed on the bits of each byte.

tags.name: norm:vclmul_op
tags.text:
Each 64-bit element in the vs2 vector register is carry-less multiplied by
either each 64-bit element in vs1 (vector-vector), or the 64-bit value
from integer register rs1 (vector-scalar). The result is the least
significant 64 bits of the carry-less product.

tags.name: norm:vclmul_sewn64_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 64

tags.name: norm:vclmulh_op
tags.text:
Each 64-bit element in the vs2 vector register is carry-less multiplied by
either each 64-bit element in vs1 (vector-vector), or the 64-bit value
from integer register rs1 (vector-scalar). The result is the most
significant 64 bits of the carry-less product.

tags.name: norm:vclmulh_sewn64_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 64

tags.name: norm:vclz-v_op
tags.text: A leading zero count is performed on each element.

tags.name: norm:vclz-v_op_zeroinput
tags.text: The result for zero-valued inputs is the value SEW.

tags.name: norm:vcpop-v_op
tags.text: A population count is performed on each element.

tags.name: norm:vctz-v_op
tags.text: A trailing zero count is performed on each element.

tags.name: norm:vghsh-vv_sewn32_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 32

tags.name: norm:vghsh-vv_op
tags.text:
This instruction treats all of the inputs and outputs as 128-bit polynomials and
performs operations over GF[2].
It produces the next partial hash (Y~i+1~) by adding the current partial
hash (Y~i~) to the cipher text block (X~i~) and then multiplying (over GF(2^128^))
this sum by the Hash Subkey (H).

tags.name: norm:vghsh-vv_op_gf
tags.text:
The multiplication over GF(2^128^) is a carry-less multiply of two 128-bit polynomials
modulo GHASH's irreducible polynomial (x^128^ + x^7^ + x^2^ + x + 1).

tags.name: norm:vghsh-vv_op_coeff
tags.text:
The NIST specification (see <<zvkg>>) orders the coefficients from left to right x~0~x~1~x~2~...x~127~
for a polynomial x~0~ + x~1~u +x~2~ u^2^ + ... + x~127~u^127^. This can be viewed as a collection of
byte elements in memory with the byte containing the lowest coefficients (i.e., 0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7)
residing at the lowest memory address. Since the bits in the bytes are reversed,
this instruction internally performs bit swaps within bytes to put the bits in the standard ordering
(e.g., 7,6,5,4,3,2,1,0).

tags.name: norm:vghsh-vv_exeindepdata
tags.text:
This instruction must always be implemented such that its execution latency does not depend
on the data being operated upon.

tags.name: norm:vgmul-vv_sewn32_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 32

tags.name: norm:vgmul-vv_op
tags.text:
This instruction treats all of the inputs and outputs as 128-bit polynomials and
performs operations over GF[2].
It produces the product over GF(2^128^) of the two 128-bit inputs.

tags.name: norm:vgmul-vv_op_gf
tags.text:
The multiplication over GF(2^128^) is a carry-less multiply of two 128-bit polynomials
modulo GHASH's irreducible polynomial (x^128^ + x^7^ + x^2^ + x + 1).

tags.name: norm:vgmul-vv_op_coeff
tags.text:
The NIST specification (see <<zvkg>>) orders the coefficients from left to right x~0~x~1~x~2~...x~127~
for a polynomial x~0~ + x~1~u +x~2~ u^2^ + ... + x~127~u^127^. This can be viewed as a collection of
byte elements in memory with the byte containing the lowest coefficients (i.e., 0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7)
residing at the lowest memory address. Since the bits in the bytes are reversed,
This instruction internally performs bit swaps within bytes to put the bits in the standard ordering
(e.g., 7,6,5,4,3,2,1,0).

tags.name: norm:vgmul-vv_exeindepdata
tags.text:
This instruction must always be implemented such that its execution latency does not depend
on the data being operated upon.

tags.name: norm:vrev8-v_op
tags.text: A byte reversal is performed on each element of vs2, effectively performing an endian swap.

tags.name: norm:vrol_op
tags.text:
The elements in vs2 are rotated left by the rotate amount specified by either
the corresponding elements of vs1 (vector-vector), or integer register rs1
(vector-scalar).
Only the low log2(SEW) bits of the rotate-amount value are used, all other
bits are ignored.

tags.name: norm:vror_op
tags.text:
The elements in vs2 are rotated right by the rotate amount specified by either
the corresponding elements of vs1 (vector-vector), integer register rs1
(vector-scalar), or an immediate value (vector-immediate).
Only the low log2(SEW) bits of the rotate-amount value are used, all other
bits are ignored.

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_Zvknha_sewn32_rsv
tags.text: zvknha: SEW is any value other than 32

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_Zvknha_sewn32_rsv
tags.text: zvknha: SEW is any value other than 32

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_Zvknhb_sewn32or64_rsv
tags.text: zvknhb: SEW is any value other than 32 or 64

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_Zvknhb_sewn32or64_rsv
tags.text: zvknhb: SEW is any value other than 32 or 64

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_vdoverlapvs1vs2_rsv
tags.text: The vd register group overlaps with either vs1 or vs2

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_vdoverlapvs1vs2_rsv
tags.text: The vd register group overlaps with either vs1 or vs2

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_op
tags.text:
Two words of vs1 are processed with
the 8 words of current state held in vd and vs2 to perform two
rounds of hash computation producing four words of the
next state.

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_op_sew32
tags.text: SEW=32: 2 rounds of SHA-256 compression are performed (zvknha and zvknhb)

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_op_sew64
tags.text: SEW=64: 2 rounds of SHA-512 compression are performed (zvknhb)

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_op
tags.text:
Two words of vs1 are processed with
the 8 words of current state held in vd and vs2 to perform two
rounds of hash computation producing four words of the
next state.

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_op_sew32
tags.text: SEW=32: 2 rounds of SHA-256 compression are performed (zvknha and zvknhb)

tags.name: norm:vsha2chl-vv_op_sew64
tags.text: SEW=64: 2 rounds of SHA-512 compression are performed (zvknhb)

tags.name: norm:vsha2ms-vv_Zvknha_sewn32_rsv
tags.text: zvknha: SEW is any value other than 32

tags.name: norm:vsha2ms-vv_Zvknhb_sewn32or64_rsv
tags.text: zvknhb: SEW is any value other than 32 or 64

tags.name: norm:vsha2ms-vv_vdoverlapvs1vs2_rsv
tags.text: The vd register group overlaps with either vs1 or vs2

tags.name: norm:vsha2ms-vv_op
tags.text:
Eleven of the last 16 SEW-sized message-schedule words from vd (oldest), vs2,
and vs1 (most recent) are processed to produce the
next 4 message-schedule words.

tags.name: norm:vsha2ms-vv_op_sew32
tags.text: SEW=32: Four rounds of SHA-256 message schedule expansion are performed (zvknha and zvknhb)

tags.name: norm:vsha2ms-vv_op_sew64
tags.text: SEW=64: Four rounds of SHA-512 message schedule expansion are performed (zvknhb)

tags.name: norm:vsm3c-vi_sewn32_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 32

tags.name: norm:vsm3c-vi_vdoverlapvs2_rsv
tags.text: The vd register group overlaps with the vs2 register group

tags.name: norm:vsm3c-vi_op
tags.text: Two rounds of SM3 compression are performed.

tags.name: norm:vsm3c-vi_op_sm3
tags.text:
The current state of eight 32-bit words is read in as an element group from vd. Eight 32-bit
message words are read in as an element group from vs2, although only four of them are used.
All of the 32-bit input words are byte-swapped from big endian to little endian.
These inputs are processed somewhat differently based on the round group (as specified in rnds),
and the next state is generated as an element group of eight 32-bit words.
The next state of eight 32-bit words are generated,
swapped from little endian to big endian, and are returned in
an eight-element group.

tags.name: norm:vsm3c-vi_op_rnd
tags.text:
The round number is provided by the 5-bit rnds unsigned immediate. Legal values are 0 - 31
and indicate which group of two rounds are being performed. For example, if rnds=1,
then rounds 2 and 3 are being performed.

tags.name: norm:vsm3me-vv_sewn32_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 32

tags.name: norm:vsm3me-vv_vdoverlapvs2_rsv
tags.text: The vd register group overlaps with the vs2 register group.

tags.name: norm:vsm3me-vv_op
tags.text: Eight rounds of SM3 message expansion are performed.

tags.name: norm:vsm3me-vv_op_sm3
tags.text:
The sixteen most recent 32-bit message words are read in as two
eight-element groups from vs1 and vs2. Each of these words is
swapped from big endian to little endian.
The next eight 32-bit message words are generated,
swapped from little endian to big endian, and are returned in
an eight-element group.

tags.name: norm:vsm4k-vi_sewn32_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 32

tags.name: norm:vsm4k-vi_op
tags.text: Four rounds of the SM4 Key Expansion are performed.

tags.name: norm:vsm4k-vi_op_sm4k
tags.text:
Four round keys are read in as a 4-element group from vs2. Each of the next four round keys are generated
by iteratively XORing the last three round keys with a constant that is indexed by the Round Group Number,
performing a byte-wise substitution, and then performing XORs between rotated versions of this value
and the corresponding current round key.

tags.name: norm:vsm4k-vi_op_rnd
tags.text:
The Round group number (rnd) comes from uimm[2:0]; the bits in uimm[4:3] are ignored.
Round group numbers range from 0 to 7 and indicate which
group of four round keys are being generated. Round Keys range from 0-31.
For example, if rnd=1, then round keys 4, 5, 6, and 7 are being generated.

tags.name: norm:vsm4r_sewn32_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 32

tags.name: norm:vsm4r-vs_vdoverlapvs2_rsv
tags.text: Only for the .vs form: the vd register group overlaps the vs2 register

tags.name: norm:vsm4r_op
tags.text: Four rounds of SM4 Encryption/Decryption are performed.

tags.name: norm:vsm4r_op_sm4encdec
tags.text:
The four words of current state are read as a 4-element group from vd
and the round keys are read from either the corresponding 4-element group
in vs2 (vector-vector form) or the scalar element group in vs2
(vector-scalar form).
The next four words of state are generated
by iteratively XORing the last three words of the state with
the corresponding round key, performing
a byte-wise substitution, and then performing XORs between rotated
versions of this value and the corresponding current state.

tags.name: norm:vwsll_op
tags.text:
The elements in vs2 are zero-extended to 2*SEW bits, then shifted left
by the shift amount specified by either
the corresponding elements of vs1 (vector-vector), integer register rs1
(vector-scalar), or an immediate value (vector-immediate).
Only the low log2(2*SEW) bits of the shift-amount value are used, all other
bits are ignored.

tags.name: norm:vaesdf_ops
tags.text: The InvShiftRows and InvSubBytes steps are applied to each round state element group from vd.

tags.name: norm:vaesdf_xor_form
tags.text:
This is then XORed with the round key in either the corresponding element group in vs2 (vector-vector
form) or scalar element group in vs2 (vector-scalar form).

tags.name: norm:vaesdm_ops
tags.text: The InvShiftRows and InvSubBytes steps are applied to each round state element group from vd.

tags.name: norm:vaesdm_xor_form
tags.text:
This is then XORed with the round key in either the corresponding element group in vs2 (vector-vector
form) or the scalar element group in vs2 (vector-scalar form).

tags.name: norm:vaesdm_invmix
tags.text: InvMixColumns step.

tags.name: norm:vaesef_ops
tags.text: The SubBytes and ShiftRows steps are applied to each round state element group from vd.

tags.name: norm:vaesef_xor_form
tags.text:
This is then XORed with the round key in either the corresponding element group in vs2 (vector-vector
form) or the scalar element group in vs2 (vector-scalar form).

tags.name: norm:vaesem_ops
tags.text: The SubBytes, ShiftRows, and MixColumns steps are applied to each round state element group from vd.

tags.name: norm:vaesem_xor_form
tags.text:
This is then XORed with the round key in either the corresponding  element group in vs2 (vector-vector
form) or the scalar element group in vs2 (vector-scalar form).

tags.name: norm:vaeskf1_round
tags.text: A single round of the forward AES-128 KeySchedule is performed.

tags.name: norm:vaeskf1_wordgen
tags.text:
The next round key is generated word by word from the
current round key element group in vs2 and the immediately previous word of the
round key.

tags.name: norm:vaeskf1_uimm_src
tags.text:
ranges from 1 to 10, comes from uimm[3:0];
uimm[4] is ignored.

tags.name: norm:vaeskf1_uimm_map
tags.text:
The out-of-range uimm[3:0] values of 0 and 11-15 are mapped to in-range
values by inverting uimm[3].

tags.name: norm:vaeskf2_round
tags.text: A single round of the forward AES-256 KeySchedule is performed.

tags.name: norm:vaeskf2_wordgen
tags.text:
The next round key is generated word by word from the
previous round key element group in vd and the immediately previous word of the
round key.

tags.name: norm:vaeskf2_rcon_lsw
tags.text:
least significant word of the next round key is generated by
applying a function to the most significant word of the current round key and
then XORing the result with the round constant.

tags.name: norm:vaeskf2_rcon_func
tags.text: The round number is used to select the round constant as well as the function.

tags.name: norm:vaeskf2_uimm_src
tags.text:
ranges from 2 to 14, comes from uimm[3:0];
uimm[4] is ignored.

tags.name: norm:vaeskf2_uimm_map
tags.text:
The out-of-range uimm[3:0] values of 0-1 and 15 are mapped to in-range
values by inverting uimm[3].

tags.name: norm:vaesz_round0
tags.text: A round-0 AES block cipher operation is performed.

tags.name: norm:vaesz_vs_only
tags.text: There is only a .vs form of the instruction.

tags.name: norm:vaesz_vs2_rk
tags.text:
Vs2 holds a
scalar element group that is used
as the round key for all of the round state element groups.

normative_rules.name: Zabha_rd_sign_extension
normative_rules.chapter_name: Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations (Zabha) Extension

tags.name: norm:Zabha_rd_sign_extension
tags.text:
Byte and halfword AMOs always sign-extend the value placed in rd, and ignore
the image:../../build/images-out/stem-2aa35a881e52a6683f571d117edc7f47.svg[width=136,height=12] bits of the original value in rs2.

normative_rules.name: Zabha_amocas-BH_ignore_bits
normative_rules.chapter_name: Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations (Zabha) Extension

tags.name: norm:Zabha_amocas-BH_ignore_bits
tags.text:
The
AMOCAS.[B|H] instructions similarly ignore the image:../../build/images-out/stem-2aa35a881e52a6683f571d117edc7f47.svg[width=136,height=12]
bits of the original value in rd.

normative_rules.name: Zabha_rs1_align_addr
normative_rules.chapter_name: Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations (Zabha) Extension

tags.name: norm:Zabha_rs1_align_addr
tags.text:
Similar to the AMOs specified in the A extension, the Zabha extension mandates
that the address contained in the rs1 register must be naturally aligned to
the size of the operand.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv32_amocas-w_op
tags.text:
For RV32, AMOCAS.W atomically loads a 32-bit data value from address in rs1,
compares the loaded value to the 32-bit value held in rd, and if the comparison
is bitwise equal, then stores the 32-bit value held in rs2 to the original
address in rs1. The value loaded from memory is placed into register rd.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv32_amocas-d_op
tags.text:
For RV32, AMOCAS.D atomically loads 64-bits of a data value from address in
rs1, compares the loaded value to a 64-bit value held in a register pair
consisting of rd and rd+1, and if the comparison is bitwise equal, then
stores the 64-bit value held in the register pair rs2 and rs2+1 to the
original address in rs1. The value loaded from memory is placed into the
register pair rd and rd+1.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv32_amocas-d_frst_pair_entry_reg_even
tags.text:
The instruction requires the first register in
the pair to be even numbered; encodings with odd numbered registers specified
in rs2 and rd are reserved.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv32_amocas-d_rs2_frst_reg_x0
tags.text:
When the first register of a source register
pair is x0, then both halves of the pair read as zero.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv32_amocas-d_rd_frst_reg_x0
tags.text:
When the first
register of a destination register pair is x0, then the entire register
result is discarded and neither destination register is written.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv64_amocas-w_op
tags.text:
For RV64, AMOCAS.W atomically loads a 32-bit data value from address in
rs1, compares the loaded value to the lower 32 bits of the value held in rd,
and if the comparison is bitwise equal, then stores the lower 32 bits of the
value held in rs2 to the original address in rs1. The 32-bit value loaded
from memory is sign-extended and is placed into register rd.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv64_amocas-d_op
tags.text:
For RV64, AMOCAS.D atomically loads 64-bits of a data value from address in
rs1, compares the loaded value to a 64-bit value held in rd, and if the
comparison is bitwise equal, then stores the 64-bit value held in rs2 to the
original address in rs1. The value loaded from memory is placed into register
rd.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv64_amocas-q_op
tags.text:
AMOCAS.Q (RV64 only) atomically loads 128-bits of a data value from address in
rs1, compares the loaded value to a 128-bit value held in a register pair
consisting of rd and rd+1, and if the comparison is bitwise equal, then
stores the 128-bit value held in the register pair rs2 and rs2+1 to the
original address in rs1. The value loaded from memory is placed into the
register pair rd and rd+1.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv64_amocas-q_frst_pair_entry_reg_even
tags.text:
The instruction requires the first register in
the pair to be even numbered; encodings with odd numbered registers specified in
rs2 and rd are reserved.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv64_amocas-q_rs2_frst_reg_x0
tags.text:
When the first register of a source register pair
is x0, then both halves of the pair read as zero.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_rv64_amocas-q_rd_frst_reg_x0
tags.text:
When the first register of a
destination register pair is x0, then the entire register result is discarded
and neither destination register is written.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_amocas_rs1_addr_alignment
tags.text:
AMOCAS.W/D/Q requires that the address
held in rs1 be naturally aligned to the size of the operand (i.e., 16-byte
aligned for quadwords, eight-byte aligned for doublewords, and four-byte
aligned for words).

tags.name: norm:Zacas_amocas_mem_op_success_aq_rl
tags.text:
The memory operation performed by an
AMOCAS.W/D/Q, when successful, has acquire semantics if aq bit is 1 and has
release semantics if rl bit is 1.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_amocas_mem_op_fail_aq_rl
tags.text:
The memory operation performed by an
AMOCAS.W/D/Q, when not successful, has acquire semantics if aq bit is 1 but
does not have release semantics, regardless of rl.

tags.name: norm:Zacas_amocas_w_permission
tags.text: An AMOCAS.W/D/Q instruction always requires write permissions.

normative_rules.name: zalasr_def
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:zalasr_def
tags.text:
The Zalasr (Load-Acquire and Store-Release) extension provides load-acquire and store-release instructions in RISC-V.
These can be important for high performance designs by enabling finer-grained synchronisation than is possible with fences alone, by providing a unidirectional fence.

normative_rules.name: zalasr_builds_on_amo
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:zalasr_builds_on_amo
tags.text: The Zalasr extension builds on the atomic support provided by the Zaamo (Atomic Memory Operations), Zalrsc (Load-Reserved and Store-Conditional), and Zabha (Byte and Halfword Atomic Memory Operations) extensions by providing additional atomic operations (although it can be implemented independently of them).

normative_rules.name: zalasr_atomic_ordered
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:zalasr_atomic_ordered
tags.text: The Zalasr instructions are atomic loads and stores that support ordering annotations.

normative_rules.name: zalasr_signext_rd
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:zalasr_signext_rd
tags.text: The Zalasr instructions always sign-extend the value placed in rd

normative_rules.name: zalasr_ignore_rs2_upper
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:zalasr_ignore_rs2_upper
tags.text: ignore the upper bits of the value of rs2.

normative_rules.name: zalasr_natural_align
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:zalasr_natural_align
tags.text: The instructions in the Zalasr extension require that the address held in rs1 be naturally aligned to the size in bytes (2^width^) of the operand.

normative_rules.name: zalasr_misaligned_exception
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:zalasr_misaligned_exception
tags.text: If the address is not naturally aligned, an address-misaligned exception or an access-fault exception will be generated.

normative_rules.name: zalasr_misaligned_pma_relax
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:zalasr_misaligned_pma_relax
tags.text: The misaligned atomicity granule PMA, defined in Volume II of this manual, optionally relaxes this alignment requirement.

normative_rules.name: zalasr_misaligned_single_op
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:zalasr_misaligned_single_op
tags.text: If all accessed bytes lie within the same misaligned atomicity granule, the instruction will not raise an exception for reasons of address alignment, and the instruction will give rise to only one memory operation for the purposes of RVWMO—i.e., it will execute atomically.

normative_rules.name: ldaq_atomic_load_enc
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:ldaq_atomic_load_enc
tags.text: Encoding

normative_rules.name: ldaq_atomic_load_op
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:ldaq_atomic_load_op
tags.text: This instruction loads 2^width^ bytes of memory from rs1 atomically and writes the result into rd.

normative_rules.name: ldaq_signext_rule
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:ldaq_signext_rule
tags.text: If the size (2^width+3^) is less than XLEN, it is sign-extended to fill the destination register.

normative_rules.name: ldaq_aq_required
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:ldaq_aq_required
tags.text: This load must have the ordering annotation aq

normative_rules.name: ldaq_rl_optional
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:ldaq_rl_optional
tags.text: may have ordering annotation rl encoded in the instruction.

normative_rules.name: ldaq_rcsc_semantics
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:ldaq_rcsc_semantics
tags.text: The instruction always has an "acquire-RCsc" annotation, and if the bit rl is set the instruction has a "release-RCsc" annotation.

normative_rules.name: ldaq_no_aq_reserved
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:ldaq_no_aq_reserved
tags.text: The versions without the aq bit set are RESERVED.

normative_rules.name: ldaq_rv64_only
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:ldaq_rv64_only
tags.text: LD.{AQ, AQRL} is RV64-only.

normative_rules.name: sdrl_atomic_store_enc
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:sdrl_atomic_store_enc
tags.text: Encoding

normative_rules.name: sdrl_atomic_store_op
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:sdrl_atomic_store_op
tags.text: This instruction stores 2^width^ bytes of memory from rs1 atomically.

normative_rules.name: sdrl_rl_required
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:sdrl_rl_required
tags.text: This store must have ordering annotation rl

normative_rules.name: sdrl_aq_optional
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:sdrl_aq_optional
tags.text: may have ordering annotation aq encoded in the instruction.

normative_rules.name: sdrl_rcsc_semantics
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:sdrl_rcsc_semantics
tags.text: The instruction always has an "release-RCsc" annotation, and if the bit aq is set the instruction has a "acquire-RCsc" annotation.

normative_rules.name: sdrl_no_rl_reserved
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:sdrl_no_rl_reserved
tags.text: The versions without the rl bit set are RESERVED.

normative_rules.name: sdrl_rv64_only
normative_rules.chapter_name: Zalasr Extension for Load-Acquire and Store-Release Instructions

tags.name: norm:sdrl_rv64_only
tags.text: SD.{RL, AQRL} is RV64-only.

tags.name: norm:Zawrs_wrs-nto_stall_exec
tags.text:
Then a subsequent WRS.NTO instruction would cause the hart to temporarily
stall execution in a low-power state until a store occurs to the reservation set
or an interrupt is observed.

tags.name: norm:Zawrs_wrs-sto_stall_duration
tags.text:
WRS.STO (WRS-with-short-timeout) is
provided that works like WRS.NTO but bounds the stall duration to an
implementation-define short timeout such that the stall is terminated on the
timeout if no other conditions have occurred to terminate the stall.

tags.name: norm:Zawrs_stall_terminate
tags.text:
While stalled, an implementation is permitted to occasionally terminate the
stall and complete execution for any reason.

tags.name: norm:Zawrs_exec_resume_rules
tags.text:
WRS.NTO and WRS.STO instructions follow the rules of the WFI instruction
for resuming execution on a pending  interrupt.

tags.name: norm:Zawrs_priv_illegal_instr_excp
tags.text:
When the TW (Timeout Wait) bit in mstatus is set and WRS.NTO is executed
in any privilege mode other than M mode, and it does not complete within an
implementation-specific bounded time limit, the WRS.NTO instruction will cause
an illegal-instruction exception.

tags.name: norm:Zawrs_virtual_instr_excp
tags.text:
When executing in VS or VU mode, if the VTW bit is set in hstatus, the
TW bit in mstatus is clear, and the WRS.NTO does not complete within an
implementation-specific bounded time limit, the WRS.NTO instruction will cause
a virtual-instruction  exception.

tags.name: norm:misa_c_set_line
tags.text: MISA.C is set if the following extensions are selected:

tags.name: norm:misa_c_set_list
tags.text:
Zca and not F
Zca, Zcf and F (but not D) is specified (RV32 only)
Zca, Zcf and Zcd if D is specified (RV32 only)

this configuration excludes Zcmp, Zcmt
Zca, Zcd if D is specified (RV64 only)

this configuration excludes Zcmp, Zcmt

tags.name: norm:c-lbu_op
tags.text: loads a byte from the memory address formed by adding rs1' to the zero extended immediate uimm. The resulting byte is zero extended to XLEN bits and is written to rd'.

tags.name: norm:c-lhu_op
tags.text: loads a halfword from the memory address formed by adding rs1' to the zero extended immediate uimm. The resulting halfword is zero extended to XLEN bits and is written to rd'.

tags.name: norm:c-lh_op
tags.text: loads a halfword from the memory address formed by adding rs1' to the zero extended immediate uimm. The resulting halfword is sign extended to XLEN bits and is written to rd'.

tags.name: norm:c-sb_op
tags.text: stores the least significant byte of rs2' to the memory address formed by adding rs1' to the zero extended immediate uimm.

tags.name: norm:c-sh_op
tags.text: stores the least significant halfword of rs2' to the memory address formed by adding rs1' to the zero extended immediate uimm.

tags.name: norm:c-zext-b_op
tags.text:
zero-extends the least-significant byte of the operand to XLEN bits by inserting zeros into all of
the bits more significant than 7.

tags.name: norm:c-sext-b_op
tags.text:
sign-extends the least-significant byte in the operand to XLEN bits by copying the most-significant bit
in the byte (i.e., bit 7) to all of the more-significant bits.

tags.name: norm:c-zext-h_op
tags.text:
zero-extends the least-significant halfword of the operand to XLEN bits by inserting zeros into all of
the bits more significant than 15.

tags.name: norm:c-sext-h_op
tags.text:
sign-extends the least-significant halfword in the operand to XLEN bits by copying the most-significant bit
in the halfword (i.e., bit 15) to all of the more-significant bits.

tags.name: norm:c-zext-w_op
tags.text:
zero-extends the least-significant word of the operand to XLEN bits by inserting zeros into all of
the bits more significant than 31.

tags.name: norm:c-not_op
tags.text: takes the one's complement of rd'/rs1' and writes the result to the same register.

tags.name: norm:c-mul_op
tags.text: multiplies XLEN bits of the source operands from rsd' and rs2' and writes the lowest XLEN bits of the result to rsd'.

tags.name: norm:Zcmp_reexecute
tags.text: Correct execution requires that sp refers to idempotent memory (also see ), because the core must be able to handle traps detected during the sequence. The entire PUSH/POP sequence is re-executed after returning from the trap handler, and multiple traps are possible during the sequence.

tags.name: norm:Zcmp_trap
tags.text: If a trap occurs during the sequence then xEPC is updated with the PC of the instruction, xTVAL (if not read-only-zero) updated with the bad address if it was an access fault and xCAUSE updated with the type of trap.

tags.name: norm:interrupts_allowed_in_pushpop
tags.text: It is implementation defined whether interrupts can also be taken during the sequence execution.

tags.name: norm:Zcmp_bus_fault
tags.text: Stores may also return imprecise faults from the bus. It is platform defined whether the core implementation waits for the bus responses before continuing to the final stage of the sequence, or handles errors responses after completing the PUSH instruction.

tags.name: norm:Zcmp_push_sp_commit
tags.text: The stack pointer adjustment must only be committed only when it is certain that the entire PUSH instruction will commit.

tags.name: norm:Zcmp_pop_sp_commit
tags.text: The optional li a0, 0, stack pointer adjustment and optional ret must only be committed only when it is certain that the entire POP/POPRET instruction will commit.

tags.name: norm:cm-push_op
tags.text:
pushes (stores) the registers in reg_list to the memory below the stack pointer,
and then creates the stack frame by decrementing the stack pointer by stack_adj,
including any additional stack space requested by the value of spimm.

tags.name: norm:cm-pop_op
tags.text:
This instruction pops (loads) the registers in reg_list from stack memory,
and then adjusts the stack pointer by stack_adj.

tags.name: norm:cm-popretz_op
tags.text: pops (loads) the registers in reg_list from stack memory, adjusts the stack pointer by stack_adj, moves zero into a0 and then returns to ra.

tags.name: norm:cm-popret_op
tags.text: pops (loads) the registers in reg_list from stack memory, adjusts the stack pointer by stack_adj and then returns to ra.

tags.name: norm:cm-mvsa01_res
tags.text: For the encoding to be legal r1s' != r2s'.

tags.name: norm:cm-mvsa01_op
tags.text: moves a0 into r1s' and a1 into r2s'.  r1s' and r2s' must be different.

tags.name: norm:cm-mvsa01_sreg
tags.text: The encoding uses sreg number specifiers instead of xreg number specifiers to save encoding space.

tags.name: norm:cm-mva01s_op
tags.text: moves r1s' into a0 and r2s' into a1.

tags.name: norm:cm-mva01s_sreg
tags.text: The encoding uses sreg number specifiers instead of xreg number specifiers to save encoding space.

tags.name: norm:Zcmt_table
tags.text:
Table jump uses a 256-entry XLEN wide table in instruction memory to contain function addresses.
The table must be a minimum of 64-byte aligned.

tags.name: norm:Zcmt_endian
tags.text: Table entries follow the current data endianness. This is different from normal instruction fetch which is always little-endian.

tags.name: norm:Zcmt_entry_sz
tags.text: The base of the table is in the jvt CSR (see <<csrs-jvt>>), each table entry is XLEN bits.

tags.name: norm:Zcmt_fetch
tags.text:
For a table jump instruction, the table entry that the instruction selects is considered an extension of the instruction itself.
Hence, the execution of a table jump instruction involves two instruction fetches, the first to read the instruction (cm.jt/cm.jalt)
and the second to read from the jump vector table (JVT). Both instruction fetches are implicit reads, and both require
execute permission; read permission is irrelevant. It is recommended that the second fetch be ignored for hardware triggers and breakpoints.

tags.name: norm:Zcmt_trap
tags.text: If an exception occurs on either instruction fetch, xEPC is set to the PC of the table jump instruction,  xCAUSE is set as expected for the type of fault and xTVAL (if not set to zero) contains the fetch address which caused the fault.

tags.name: norm:jvt_reg
tags.text: The jvt register is an XLEN-bit WARL read/write register that holds the jump table configuration, consisting of the jump table base address (BASE) and the jump table mode (MODE).

tags.name: norm:jvt_reg
tags.text: The jvt register is an XLEN-bit WARL read/write register that holds the jump table configuration, consisting of the jump table base address (BASE) and the jump table mode (MODE).

tags.name: norm:jvt_op
tags.text:
If <<Zcmt>> is implemented then jvt must also be implemented, but can contain a read-only value. If jvt is writable, the set of values the register may hold can vary by implementation. The value in the BASE field must always be aligned on a 64-byte boundary.
Note that the CSR contains only bits XLEN-1 through 6 of the address base. When computing jump-table accesses, the lower six bits of base are filled with zeroes to obtain an XLEN-bit jump-table base address jvt.base that is always aligned on a 64-byte boundary.

tags.name: norm:jvt_base_vm
tags.text: jvt.base is a virtual address, whenever virtual memory is enabled.

tags.name: norm:jvt_mode_acc
tags.text: jvt.mode is a WARL field, so can only be programmed to modes which are implemented.

tags.name: norm:jvt_mode_acc
tags.text: jvt.mode is a WARL field, so can only be programmed to modes which are implemented.

tags.name: norm:cm-jt_op
tags.text: cm.jt reads an entry from the jump vector table in memory and jumps to the address that was read.

tags.name: norm:cm-jalt_op
tags.text: cm.jalt reads an entry from the jump vector table in memory and jumps to the address that was read, linking to ra.

tags.name: norm:fli-s_op
tags.text: The FLI.S instruction loads one of 32 single-precision floating-point constants, encoded in the rs1 field, into floating-point register rd. The correspondence of rs1 field values and single-precision floating-point values is shown in . FLI.S is encoded like FMV.W.X, but with rs2=1.

tags.name: norm:fli-d_op
tags.text: If the D extension is implemented, FLI.D performs the analogous operation, but loads a double-precision value into floating-point register rd. Note that entry 1 (corresponding to the minimum positive normal value) has a numerically different value for double-precision than for single-precision. FLI.D is encoded like FLI.S, but with fmt=D.

tags.name: norm:fli-q_op
tags.text: If the Q extension is implemented, FLI.Q performs the analogous operation, but loads a quad-precision value into floating-point register rd. Note that entry 1 (corresponding to the minimum positive normal value) has a numerically different value for quad-precision. FLI.Q is encoded like FLI.S, but with fmt=Q.

tags.name: norm:fli-h_op
tags.text: If the Zfh or Zvfh extension is implemented, FLI.H performs the analogous operation, but loads a half-precision floating-point value into register rd. Note that entry 1 (corresponding to the minimum positive normal value) has a numerically different value for half-precision. Furthermore, since 216 is not representable in half-precision floating-point, entry 29 in the table instead loads positive infinity—i.e., it is redundant with entry 30. FLI.H is encoded like FLI.S, but with fmt=H.

tags.name: norm:fmaxm-s_fminm-s_op
tags.text: The FMINM.S and FMAXM.S instructions are defined like the FMIN.S and FMAX.S instructions, except that if either input is NaN, the result is the canonical NaN.

tags.name: norm:fmaxm-s_fminm-s_op
tags.text: The FMINM.S and FMAXM.S instructions are defined like the FMIN.S and FMAX.S instructions, except that if either input is NaN, the result is the canonical NaN.

tags.name: norm:fmaxm-d_fminm-d_op
tags.text: If the D extension is implemented, FMINM.D and FMAXM.D instructions are analogously defined to operate on double-precision numbers.

tags.name: norm:fmaxm-d_fminm-d_op
tags.text: If the D extension is implemented, FMINM.D and FMAXM.D instructions are analogously defined to operate on double-precision numbers.

tags.name: norm:fmaxm-q_fminm-q_op
tags.text: If the Q extension is implemented, FMINM.Q and FMAXM.Q instructions are analogously defined to operate on quad-precision numbers.

tags.name: norm:fmaxm-q_fminm-q_op
tags.text: If the Q extension is implemented, FMINM.Q and FMAXM.Q instructions are analogously defined to operate on quad-precision numbers.

tags.name: norm:fmaxm-h_fminm-h_op
tags.text: If the Zfh extension is implemented, FMINM.H and FMAXM.H instructions are analogously defined to operate on half-precision numbers.

tags.name: norm:fmaxm-h_fminm-h_op
tags.text: If the Zfh extension is implemented, FMINM.H and FMAXM.H instructions are analogously defined to operate on half-precision numbers.

tags.name: norm:fround-s_op
tags.text:
The FROUND.S instruction rounds the single-precision floating-point
number in floating-point register rs1 to an integer, according to the
rounding mode specified in the instruction's rm field. It then writes
that integer, represented as a single-precision floating-point number,
to floating-point register rd

tags.name: norm:froundnx-s_op
tags.text: The FROUNDNX.S instruction is defined similarly, but it also sets the inexact exception flag if the input differs from the rounded result and is not NaN. FROUNDNX.S is encoded like FCVT.S.D, but with rs2=5.

tags.name: norm:fround-d_froundnx-d_op
tags.text: If the D extension is implemented, FROUND.D and FROUNDNX.D instructions are analogously defined to operate on double-precision numbers. They are encoded like FCVT.D.S, but with rs2=4 and 5, respectively,

tags.name: norm:fround-d_froundnx-d_op
tags.text: If the D extension is implemented, FROUND.D and FROUNDNX.D instructions are analogously defined to operate on double-precision numbers. They are encoded like FCVT.D.S, but with rs2=4 and 5, respectively,

tags.name: norm:fround-q_froundnx-q_op
tags.text: If the Q extension is implemented, FROUND.Q and FROUNDNX.Q instructions are analogously defined to operate on quad-precision numbers. They are encoded like FCVT.Q.S, but with rs2=4 and 5, respectively,

tags.name: norm:fround-q_froundnx-q_op
tags.text: If the Q extension is implemented, FROUND.Q and FROUNDNX.Q instructions are analogously defined to operate on quad-precision numbers. They are encoded like FCVT.Q.S, but with rs2=4 and 5, respectively,

tags.name: norm:fround-h_froundnx-h_op
tags.text: If the Zfh extension is implemented, FROUND.H and FROUNDNX.H instructions are analogously defined to operate on half-precision numbers. They are encoded like FCVT.H.S, but with rs2=4 and 5, respectively,

tags.name: norm:fround-h_froundnx-h_op
tags.text: If the Zfh extension is implemented, FROUND.H and FROUNDNX.H instructions are analogously defined to operate on half-precision numbers. They are encoded like FCVT.H.S, but with rs2=4 and 5, respectively,

tags.name: norm:fcvtmod-w-d_op
tags.text: The FCVTMOD.W.D instruction is defined similarly to the FCVT.W.D instruction, with the following differences. FCVTMOD.W.D always rounds towards zero. Bits 31:0 are taken from the rounded, unbounded two’s complement result, then sign-extended to XLEN bits and written to integer register rd. ±∞ and NaN are converted to zero.

tags.name: norm:fcvtmod-w-d_flags
tags.text: Floating-point exception flags are raised the same as they would be for FCVT.W.D with the same input operand.

tags.name: norm:fcvtmod-w-d_rsw
tags.text:
It
is encoded like FCVT.W.D, but with the rs2 field set to 8 and the rm
field set to 1 (RTZ). Other rm values are reserved.

tags.name: norm:fmvh-x-d_op
tags.text: For RV32 only, if the D extension is implemented, the FMVH.X.D instruction moves bits 63:32 of floating-point register rs1 into integer register rd. It is encoded in the OP-FP major opcode with funct3=0, rs2=1, and funct7=1110001.

tags.name: norm:fmvp-d-x_op
tags.text: For RV32 only, if the D extension is implemented, the FMVP.D.X instruction moves a double-precision number from a pair of integer registers into a floating-point register. Integer registers rs1 and rs2 supply bits 31:0 and 63:32, respectively; the result is written to floating-point register rd. FMVP.D.X is encoded in the OP-FP major opcode with funct3=0 and funct7=1011001.

tags.name: norm:fmvh-x-q_op
tags.text: For RV64 only, if the Q extension is implemented, the FMVH.X.Q instruction moves bits 127:64 of floating-point register rs1 into integer register rd. It is encoded in the OP-FP major opcode with funct3=0, rs2=1, and funct7=1110011.

tags.name: norm:fmvp-q-x_op
tags.text: For RV64 only, if the Q extension is implemented, the FMVP.Q.X instruction moves a double-precision number from a pair of integer registers into a floating-point register. Integer registers rs1 and rs2 supply bits 63:0 and 127:64, respectively; the result is written to floating-point register rd. FMVP.Q.X is encoded in the OP-FP major opcode with funct3=0 and funct7=1011011.

tags.name: norm:fsh_flh_op
tags.text: New 16-bit variants of LOAD-FP and STORE-FP instructions are added, encoded with a new value for the funct3 width field.

tags.name: norm:fsh_flh_op
tags.text: New 16-bit variants of LOAD-FP and STORE-FP instructions are added, encoded with a new value for the funct3 width field.

tags.name: norm:fsh_flh_atomic_align
tags.text: FLH and FSH are only guaranteed to execute atomically if the effective address is naturally aligned.

tags.name: norm:fsh_flh_atomic_align
tags.text: FLH and FSH are only guaranteed to execute atomically if the effective address is naturally aligned.

tags.name: norm:fsh_flh_bits_maintained
tags.text: FLH and FSH do not modify the bits being transferred; in particular, the payloads of non-canonical NaNs are preserved. FLH NaN-boxes the result written to rd, whereas FSH ignores all but the lower 16 bits in rs2.

tags.name: norm:fsh_flh_bits_maintained

tags.text:
FADD.S and FMUL.S
perform single-precision floating-point addition and multiplication
respectively, between rs1 and rs2

tags.name: norm:Zfh_computational_instrs
tags.text: The half-precision floating-point computational instructions are defined analogously to their single-precision counterparts, but operate on half-precision operands and produce half-precision results.

tags.name: norm:FP_nontransfer_instrs_improper_nan-boxed_input
tags.text: Apart from transfer operations described in the previous paragraph, all other floating-point operations on narrower n-bit operations, n<FLEN, check if the input operands are correctly NaN-boxed, i.e., all upper FLEN-n bits are 1. If so, the n least-significant bits of the input are used as the input value, otherwise the input value is treated as an n-bit canonical NaN.

tags.name: norm:ieee_std_subnormal
tags.text: Operations on subnormal numbers are handled in accordance with the IEEE 754-2008 standard.

tags.name: norm:ieee_std_tininess
tags.text: In the parlance of the IEEE standard, tininess is detected after rounding.

frm

000|RNE|Round to Nearest, ties to Even¶001|RTZ|Round towards Zero¶010|RDN|Round Down (towards −∞)¶011|RUP|Round Up (towards +∞)¶100|RMM|Round to Nearest, ties to Max Magnitude¶101||Reserved for future use.¶110||Reserved for future use.¶111|DYN|In instruction’s rm field, selects dynamic rounding mode; In Rounding Mode register, reserved.
===

tags.name: norm:roundingmode_dynamic
tags.text:
Floating-point operations use either a static rounding mode encoded in
the instruction, or a dynamic rounding mode held in frm. Rounding
modes are encoded as shown in <<norm:dyn_round_enc>>. A value of 111 in the
instruction's rm field selects the dynamic rounding mode held in
frm

tags.name: norm:fcvt-h-w_fcvt-h-l_op
tags.text:
FCVT.H.W or FCVT.H.L converts a 32-bit or 64-bit signed
integer, respectively, into a half-precision floating-point number

tags.name: norm:fcvt_round
tags.text: All floating-point to integer and integer to floating-point conversion instructions round according to the rm field. A floating-point register can be initialized to floating-point positive zero using FCVT.S.W rd, x0, which will never set any exception flags.

tags.name: norm:fcvt_nx
tags.text: All floating-point conversion instructions set the Inexact exception flag if the rounded result differs from the operand value and the Invalid exception flag is not set.

tags.name: norm:fcvt_unrepresentable_nv
tags.text:
If the rounded result is not representable in the
destination format, it is clipped to the nearest value and the invalid
flag is set

tags.name: norm:ieee_std_subnormal
tags.text: Operations on subnormal numbers are handled in accordance with the IEEE 754-2008 standard.

tags.name: norm:ieee_std_tininess
tags.text: In the parlance of the IEEE standard, tininess is detected after rounding.

tags.text: Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.H, FSGNJN.H, and FSGNJX.H are defined analogously to the single-precision sign-injection instruction.

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-d_fsgnjn-d_fsgnjx-d_op
tags.text: Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.D, FSGNJN.D, and FSGNJX.D are defined analogously to the single-precision sign-injection instruction.

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-h_fsgnjn-h_fsgnjx-h_op
